quotes.quote: We investigate the intrinsic Baldwin effect (Beff) of the broad H and H emission lines for six Type 1 active galactic nuclei (AGNs) with different broad line characteristics: two Seyfert 1 ( NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 ), two AGNs with double-peaked broad line profiles ( 3C 390.3 and Arp 102B ), one narrow line Seyfert 1 ( Ark 564 ), and one high-luminosity quasar with highly red asymmetric broad line profiles ( E1821+643 ). We find that a significant intrinsic Beff was present in all Type 1 AGNs in our sample. Moreover, we do not see strong differences in intrinsic Beff slopes in different types of AGNs, which probably have different physical properties such as inclination, broad line region (BLR) geometry, or accretion rate.

The sample contains six long-term monitored AGNs with different spectral characteristics and subclassifications: Seyfert 1 AGNs NGC 5548 and NGC 4151 , which showed strong variability in the broad spectral lines (full width at half maximum (FWHM) 5000-7000 \ km s^ 1 ) and continuum flux see, e.g., sh04,sh08,sh10a . Moreover, their broad line profiles exhibit noticeable change in line shape and intensity, resulting in periods when these AGNs almost show the characteristics of Seyfert 2 very weak broad lines; see sh04,sh08,sh10a ; Double-peaked broad line AGNs 3C 390.3 and Arp 102B , whose broad Balmer lines show two distinguished peaks with very large overall width (FWHM 10000 \ kms^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4151 as a Seyfert 1 AGN whose H-line data reveal significant 5.4-, 8.3-, and 14-year period clusters interpreted as coupled oscillatory behavior.

quotes.quote: The characteristics of these objects encompasses the entire range of type 1 AGN activity (see Table  tab:data ): from Seyfert 1 objects NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 as prototypes of spectral type variation (from Seyfert 1.0 to 1.8), a very broad line and radio loud object 3C 390.3, then Arp 102B that is considered as a LINER and archetypical disc accretion emitter due to extremely broad, double peaked Balmer lines, and finally radio quiet but optically powerful near-by quasar E1821+643.

NGC 4151. The data set of the H line is long enough, for our hybrid method to clearly detect periods at significance level p<0.00001 . Obtained autocorrelation map (see Fig. fig:per41 ) revealed three clusters of significant periods (large correlation coefficients) at 5.4, 8.3 and 14 yr in cadence. Phase curves of the continuum and H line are quite similar to phase curve of the H line but their main loops are open.
Cadenced topology of three detected periods in the H line of NGC 4151 led us to suspect that periodic signals can be non-linearly coupled.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4151 to set AGN polarization-reverberation models, adopting observed ionized-region and dusty-region size constraints for its circumnuclear geometry.

quotes.quote: The model parameters are adjusted to the observational appearance of the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 4151. We modeled scattering-induced polarization and tested different geometries for the circumnuclear dust component. Our tests included the effects of clumpiness and different dust prescriptions.

For the ionized regions we assumed an inner radius of 8 light days such as found by 2012ApJ...749..148G in the case of NGC 4151, but without detailed modeling. In the case of the circumnuclear dusty regions, we adopted the observational constraints derived by near-IR observation of NGC 4151, namely 0.061 pc, according to 2013ApJ...775L..36K .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4151 as an archetypal, bright Seyfert galaxy with an optically and UV-measured black-hole mass, extensively observed optical polarization, and strong broad-line variability.

quotes.quote: NGC4151 is a 1.5 Seyfert galaxy situated at z=0.0033 1991S&T....82Q.621D , which is sometimes considered to be the archetypal Seyfert 1 galaxy 2008A&A...486...99S,2010A&A...509A.106S . It is one of the brightest Type-1 AGN in the X-ray and ultraviolet band, and its bolometric luminosity is of the order of 5d43 erg 2002ApJ...579..530W . The mass of its central supermassive black hole was estimated by optical and ultraviolet reverberation techniques and is estimated at 4.5d7 2006ApJ...651..775B . Since NGC 4151 stands out thanks to its high fluxes and proximity, its optical polarization was extensively observed (see 2016A&A...591A..23M ). The averaged 4000 - 8000 continuum polarization is below 1 , with a polarization position angle parallel to the radio axis of the system.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4151 as a narrow-line Seyfert I example with measured black hole mass, X-ray luminosity, corona location, and required magnetic field estimate.

quotes.quote: Take the narrow line Seyfert I galaxy NGC 4151 as an example. Here the black hole mass is M=(4.5 0.5) 10^ 7 M_ 2006ApJ...651..775B , so r_g=6.7 10^ 12 cm 2.2 10^2 light seconds, and L_ Edd =5.7 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 . The X-ray luminosity L_X 5 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 0.01L_ Edd 1996MNRAS.283..193Z . The corona is located at 6r_g based on Fe line reverberation mapping 2012MNRAS.422..129Z . From Equation ( eq:P_dissipation ) we need a magnetic field of B 0.3 B_ Edd 5.4 10^4 G.

sources.summary: The paper fits NuSTAR spectra of NGC 4151 with the TCAF model, deriving accretion-flow properties and an average central black-hole mass near 3.03e7 solar masses.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we study spectral properties of NGC 4151 with TCAF using three NuSTAR observations during 2012. For the first time, we use TCAF model directly in XSPEC to fit the AGN data. We obtain the accretion flow properties as extracted by TCAF. For the best fit, we also use an additional PL component to consider the possible contribution from the jet, which is not currently incorporated in TCAF. The average of the estimated mass of the central black hole appears to be 3.03^ +0.26 _ 0.26 10^7 M_ which is in the same ball park as obtained by previous workers from other considerations.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4151 H I widths mostly consistent with earlier values, noting that baseline fitting can explain small measurement discrepancies.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151: Our measurements of W _ 50 and W _ 20 are approximately consistent with the values reported by . The small discrepancies of 3 10 km s ^ 1 most likely come from the difference in spectral resolution, channel span, and channel spacing. For relatively flat baselines such as that present in our spectrum of NGC 4151, low-order polynomial fits can possibly introduce low-level sinusoidal structure in the baseline. This can affect subsequent measurements and/or fits to the emission profile, and can thus result in small discrepancies in reported measurements. The NRAO 91 m telescope employed by produced a spectrum for NGC 4151 with a resolution of 11 km s ^ 1 over 192 channels.

sources.summary: The paper applies optical/IR footprint-line photoionization modeling to NGC 4151, matching observed fluxes and deriving an X-ray emission-line gas mass comparable to Chandra measurements.

quotes.quote: We use Cloudy photoionisation models to predict the flux profiles for optical/IR emissions lines that trace the footprint of X-ray gas in NGC 4151, such as [ Fe X ] 6375 and [ Si X ] 1.43 m.

As a test case, we use the footprints to quantify the properties of the X-ray outflow in the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 4151. To confirm the accuracy of our method, we compare our model predictions to the measured flux from archival STIS spectra and previous ground-based studies, and the results are in good agreement. We also use our X-ray footprint method to predict the mass profile for the X-ray emission-line gas in NGC 4151 and derive a total spatially-integrated X-ray mass of 7.8( 2.1) 10^ 5 M_ , in comparison to 5.4( 1.1) 10^ 5 M_ measured from the Chandra X-ray analysis.
It would be interesting to obtain observations with higher spectral resolution for NGC 4151, which would allow us to compute the predicted mass outflow rates and again test our proposed methodology.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4151 as a nearby X-ray bright Seyfert benchmark for indirect black-hole-mass methods, finding X-ray scaling and one M-sigma relation accurate while the fundamental plane is imprecise.

quotes.quote: Since it is difficult to evaluate the accuracy and precision of these indirect methods, we utilize the nearby X-ray bright Seyfert galaxy , whose \ has been tightly constrained with several independent direct methods, as a laboratory to assess the reliability of three indirect methods that have been applied to obscured AGN. All three, the X-ray scaling method, the fundamental plane of black hole activity, and the M-- correlation, yield \ values consistent with those inferred from direct methods and can therefore be considered accurate. However, only the X-ray scaling method and the M-- correlation are precise because the substantial scatter in the fundamental plane of BH activity allows only for crude estimates. Of the four M-- correlations we used, only the one from Kormendy and Ho yields a value consistent with the dynamical estimates.
In this section, we estimate the black hole mass of NGC 4151 using models for the M-- \ relationship found in the four papers we describe in the rest of this section.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4151 as an AGN example where infrared time delays vary with UV luminosity and unusually large L-to-K lag differences appeared during a rare high state.

quotes.quote: In an individual object (for example, NGC 4151) the time delay varies with the mean L_ UV level. This is because of the destruction and replenishment of dust as the high-energy flux varies. These variations of the IR time delay indicate a lag in changes in the size of the hot dust region of about a few years behind L_ UV variations Oknyansky2008, Kishimoto2013 .

Only for 2 objects, GQ Comae and NGC 4151, was a difference of about a factor of three between lags in the L and K bands observed. For NGC 4151 such a big difference was observed only during the unusually long, very high state which was the only such one during more than a century of monitoring Oknyansky2013 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4151 as the Type I AGN comparison, fitting its SED and finding all three torus models indicate a direct view of the central source.

quotes.quote: We also employed, for comparison purposes, photometric points for NGC 4151 and NGC 1068 as representatives of Type I and II AGN, respectively.

Test case with NGC 4151 and NGC 1068 In order to test the fitting procedure with clumpy , cat3d-wind and skirtor , we first fit the SED of the archetypal Seyfert 1 and 2 galaxies NGC 4151 and NGC 1068, respectively. To compose the SED, photometric points in the optical, NIR, and MIR were extracted from Gaia, 2MASS, and WISE, respectively, through the VizieR photometry tool. The best SED fitting obtained for NGC 4151 and NGC 1068 are illustrated in Figure fig:sedst1e2clumpy for clumpy , Figure fig:sedst1e2skir for skirtor and in Figure fig:sedst1e2cat for cat3d-wind . In order to evaluate the consistency of the results in relation to the spectral type, the parameters and i were analyzed. For NGC 4151, as only photometric points were used, the problem becomes more degenerate and a group of templates fit in the same model present some opposition with respect to BIC.
For NGC 4151, clumpy , skirtor and cat3d-wind suggest a viewing angle such that the observer is facing the central source.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4151 as one of the two highest-significance hard-X-ray AGN point-source candidates and reports a 2.9 sigma observed significance.

quotes.quote: From the point source search, the top two sources that result in the highest levels of significance are NGC 1068 and NGC 4151. A previous analysis reported the observation on NGC 1068 and the results of this analysis are compatible with the evidence previously found. Figure fig:ra_dec_scans shows the local probability around the location of the two most significant sources obtained from the point source search.

The sources found in the point source analysis with the highest significance are Seyfert galaxies that are highly obscured. NGC 1068 has been reported in a previous IceCube study and hence, not considered in the final estimation of the significance evaluation. NGC 4151 is observed at a significance level of 2.9 . From the stacking analysis, we do not find any evidence of significant neutrino emission from any of the hard X-ray AGN classes tested but the second analysis where we searched for emission from individual sources resulted in two sources showing potential neutrino emission.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4151 prefers the hybrid model, with improved high-energy and 6-7 keV residuals, a neutral-component Compton hump, and low Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: We find that 12 out of the 18 objects (Mrk 335, Fairall 9, Mrk 1040, NGC 1365, Ark 120, NGC 3227, NGC 3783, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, IRAS 13197-1627, NGC 5548, and NGC 7469) prefer the hybrid model (i.e. Residuals of hybrid model of NGC 4151 and Ark 120 shows a better fit above 10 keV and between 6-7 keV, compared to the neutral and ionized models. Note that residuals of NGC 4151 around 6-7 keV are bigger in NuSTAR data, possible due to the cross calibration between the satellites.

The compton hump is fitted by the neutral component except for three sources (NGC 1365, NGC 3783, and NGC 4151). The lower Eddington ratios are observed for the sources that prefer the hybrid model, being Mrk 140, NGC 3227, NGC 4151 and IRAS 13197-1627 the sources with the lowest Eddington ratios, even considering their uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper presents sub-parsec VLBA imaging and radio SED results for NGC 4151, describing its central radio morphology and spectral shape in the FRAMEx AGN sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 [ 12 ^ h 10 ^ m 32 58201, + 39 24 21 063 ] : All four band images revealed a bright central sub-parsec scale emission with a ``candle flame'' shape outflow in the northeast direction. The elongated structure is visible in all four band images, even in high-resolution, K band sub-parsec ( 0.1 pc) spatial scale. The interesting jet/outflow structures of NGC 4151 were previously reported in various sub-parsec to parsec scale VLBI and e-MERLIN studies Ulvestad_2005AJ....130..936U, Williams_2017MNRAS.472.3842W, Williams_2020MNRAS.495.3079W . While plotting SEDs using only C and X band peak intensities and integrated flux densities, we found flat to slightly steeper spectra, suggesting that the central region is a compact point source. Four frequency SED model using all peak intensities showed a GHZ peaked spectrum with flatter in lower frequency regime and a steeper spectrum in higher frequency as K band peak emission is relatively weak.

sources.summary: The paper tests a claimed ROSAT filament from NGC 4151 to a BL Lac object and concludes the connection is not significant.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 Arp (1997) suggests that a filament connects this well-studied Seyfert galaxy to a z=0.615 BL Lac object 5 arcmin to the N in a ROSAT HRI image. Arp binned the HRI data into 10-arcsec (20-pixel) square bins; the off-source background is then 1.21 counts pixel ^ 1 . It is not clear what Arp means when he says that he smoothed the data with a ` 5 Gaussian'; a = 5 arcsec Gaussian is very small given this binning scheme while a = 5 pixel Gaussian is very large, causing the two sources to overlap. I obtain images quite similar to those of Arp (1997) if I smooth with a = 3 pixel Gaussian (Fig. \ ngc4151 ).
The connection seen in Fig. \ ngc4151 is therefore not significant.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4151 as an AGN with reconstructed ultraviolet velocity-delay maps, where line delays and velocity envelopes support virial broad-line motions and a black-hole mass near 10^7 solar masses.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:4151 , the velocity-delay map for \ 1550 and \ 1640 emission was reconstructed from 44 IUE spectra of NGC 4151 . While spanning only 36 days, this campaign recorded favorable continuum variations, including a bumpy exponential decline followed by a rapid rise, which were sufficient to support echo mapping on delays from 0 to 20 days. Although a strong \ absorption feature obliterates the delay structure at small velocities, it is clear that a wide range of velocities is present at small delays, and and smaller range at larger delays. The dashed curves in Fig. fig:4151 give escape velocity envelopes v = 2GM/ for masses 0.5, 1.0, and 2.0 10^ 7 M _ . This clearly supports the hypothesis of virial motions, and suggests a mass of order 10^ 7 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4151 as an active Seyfert with a nearby powerful X-ray/radio BL Lac source, an apparent X-ray filament, and an additional IXO-associated galaxy chain.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151: There is a powerful X-ray source only 4.9 arcmin N of this active Seyfert galaxy. It is also a strong radio source (de Ruiter, Arp, Willis 1977) but, surprisingly, quite faint optically. Later it was shown that there was an X-ray filament apparently linking this z = 0.615 BL Lac quasar to the nucleus of NGC 4151 (Arp 1997, Fig. 17). The object should have been included in the CP02 catalog but they centered their field to the SW of NGC 4151, just leaving it out of their frame. As a result this important BL Lac object was not listed as an IXO.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4151 as a reverberation-mapped AGN with an HST-resolvable black-hole sphere of influence, observed with STIS for stellar dynamics during a faint nuclear state.

quotes.quote: Of all the reverberation-mapped AGNs, only NGC 3227 and NGC 4151 might be expected to have a black hole radius of influence that is spatially resolvable with HST . These two AGNs are thus good candidates for measuring their black hole masses by stellar dynamical methods. A serious problem is that the AGN nuclear light swamps the stellar light, making the stellar absorption features difficult to detect. We have attempted to mitigate this problem by obtaining HST STIS spectra of the Ca ii triplet in the nucleus of NGC 4151 while the active nucleus was in a faint state in 2003 December. We are now attempting to model the centeral stellar dynamics from these data, and thus effect for the first time a direct comparison between the RM and stellar dynamical methods of black hole mass measurement.

sources.summary: The paper reports removing NGC 4151 nuclear emission, using F547M long and short exposures for accurate nuclear subtraction, avoiding NLR correction, and converting its magnitude to F606W.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 -- Nuclear emission features in the \ spectrum were removed by combining model nuclear emission with a seeing star. Long and short exposure images for this object were obtained with the F547M filter. This allowed an accurate subtraction of the nucleus. Also this filter excludes most of the optical emission lines so no correction was applied for the NLR. The magnitude in table tab:bulgetab has been converted to the F606W bandpass using CALCPHOT assuming the spectrum is well represented by the Sb template in the SYNPHOT package. This is the most discrepant point in figure fig:usthemmag , for which mcluredunlop02 used the bulge-disk decomposition of bagbagand based on ground based photographic imaging.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4151 as a bright Seyfert AGN whose Spitzer spectrum shows hot dust, probable silicate emission, strong high-ionization lines, and no PAH emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 is a classical Seyfert galaxy and is the optically-brightest example of an AGN with a visible broad line region. The spectrum in Figure 1 shows a flattening of the continuum at about 20 , implying a conspicuous hot dust component. There are weak excesses above a smooth continuum probably caused by both 10 and 18 silicate emission features. The high ionization lines of [ NeV ] and [ OIV ] are very strong, indicating that the narrow line region is primarily ionized by the AGN ( stu99 , ale99 ). There is no evidence of PAH emission features.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4151 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with complex X-ray absorption, including a transient 8.5-9 keV feature interpreted as highly ionized iron or an outflowing Fe blend.

quotes.quote: Outflow in NGC 4151 The Seyfert 1 galaxy 1 is characterized by complex X-ray absorption, well described by a dual absorber, composed of a uniform mildly ionized gas and a cold system that partially covers the central source. However, in one of the 5 observations, the spectrum shows two peculiar features. An absorption feature is detected around 8.5-9 keV with a statistical significance of 99.96 . This feature can be fitted either with an absorption edge at E =8.62^ +0.34 _ 0.52 keV with optical depth =0.06 0.03 or with an absorption line with 9.5^ +1.3 _ 0.6 keV, width =0.95^ +1.2 _ 0.7 keV and EW= 200 eV. In the first case, we associate the feature to highly ionized iron at rest, like FeXXII-FeXXIII ( E_ rest =8.4-8.5 keV).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4151 as a representative Type 1 AGN and uses its mid-infrared line ratios to infer a small NLR contribution to 25 micron emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 and NGC 4151 are two of the closest and most studied active galaxies. Respectively these are quite often used as the representative Type-2 and Type 1 AGN, corresponding to strongly obscured, edge-on, and lightly obscured, face-on AGN in the unified model. NGC 4151 appears to the lower left of the region occupied by the models. The observed mid-IR [ ] and [ ] lines give a density of 10^3 \ (figure fig:dens ), indicating the ratios are less by 1.0 to 1.5 dex. This suggests that the NLR contributes only about 3 -- 10 to the total 25 \ emission, depending on the ionization state of the gas.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4151 optical emission-line reverberation, arguing that cross-correlation underestimates its BLR size and that variable BLR size is not required by the data.

quotes.quote: The time behaviour of NGC 4151 in the H and H lines is modelled on the basis of the observational data by Kaspi  et al. (1996) and the theoretical BLR model by Shevchenko (1984, 1985). The values of the BLR parameters are estimated that allow to judge on the size and physical characteristics of the BLR. The small size of the BLR, as determined by the cross-correlation method from the data of Kaspi  et al. (1996), is shown to be an artifact of this method. So, the hypothesis that the BLR size varies in time is not necessitated by the observational data.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4151 optical/UV time lags marginally consistent with standard accretion-disk reprocessing, with longer wavelength variations lagging shorter wavelength variations.

quotes.quote: It can be seen from columns (4) and (5) of Table 3 that the measured time lags are marginally consistent with those predicted by the standard accretion disks for NGC 4151 and NGC 7469. This implies that the optical and UV emissions are likely to be the reprocessed X-rays for NGC 4151 and NGC 7469. In addition, the time lags decrease slightly as spin parameter a_ increases.
However, the longer wavelength variations lag the shorter wavelength variations for NGC 4151 and NGC 7469.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4151 as a well-studied Seyfert whose low-flux state revealed ILR emission and allowed a 0.1 pc gas-distance estimate applicable to X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: Two extensively studied sources, NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 2008A&A...488...67D recently underwent an historically broad-band low flux. In those occasions, the UV broad lines were absent, revealing the presence of lines with smaller width (from an intermediate line region, ILR), consistently with that measured for the absorption lines. Interpreting this width as due to Keplerian broadening, a distance of 0.1 pc for both the emitting and absorbing gas has been derived in NGC 4151 2007ApJ...659..250C . This estimate could be readily applied to the X-ray band absorbing gas, as this shared the same parameters as the UV absorbing/emitting gas.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4151 as the first AGN with Fe K-band reverberation lags, whose lag-energy spectrum traces a relativistically broadened iron line with distinct blue-horn and red-wing structure.

quotes.quote: A major recent development was the discovery that the lags in NGC 4151 which has an energy dependence that traces the shape of a relativistically-broad iron line ( 2012MNRAS.422..129Z ). This is a clear and dramatic confirmation of a central prediction of the relativistic disk-reflection paradigm. The observed lag in this case is an extension of the soft reverberation lags seen in many objects ( 2009Natur.459..540F, 2010MNRAS.401.2419Z and later work), and it is separate from the low-frequency featureless continuum lag. In addition, the `line' seen in the lag-energy spectrum of NGC 4151 has a stronger blue horn at long time-scales and a prominent red wing at short time-scales, in a striking match to the expectation of a line emitted from a disk and distorted by relativistic effects ( 1989MNRAS.238..729F ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4151 as a resolved Seyfert test case for radiation pressure confinement, comparing luminosity, slit-averaged observations, and observed density-sensitive line ratios with model expectations.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4151 as a well-sampled Seyfert 1 SED object, treating some low-resolution near-infrared fluxes as upper limits in torus-model tests.

quotes.quote: For the galaxies NGC 1068, NGC 3081, NGC 3227 and NGC 4151, the data are the same reported in Alonso11 and Ramos11 , and for NGC 1365, in Alonso12 , with the following exceptions: In the case of NGC 3227, we discarded the 8.99 VISIR/VLT flux from Honig10 and the 11.2 MICHELLE/Gemini North flux from Ramos09 because they appear contaminated by [ Ar III ] and PAH 11.3 emission, respectively.
