For 9 of the 13 AGN, the measurements or limits on \ are fully consistent with . These are NGC 3783, NGC 4151, NGC 4593, NGC 5548, Mrk 509, 3C 273, IRAS 13349+2438, NGC 4051 and IC 4329A.
Fig. fig:comparison shows that in many of the sources with the best constraints on \ (e.g. NGC 4151, NGC 5548, NGC 3783), \ is well matched to .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4151 as the Seyfert-galaxy basis for a receding-torus radiative-transfer model, adopting its dust, kinematic, luminosity, and X-ray-polarization constraints.

quotes.quote: Using a Monte Carlo approach, we simulate the radiative transfer between the multiple components of an AGN adopting model constraints from the bright Seyfert galaxy NGC 4151.
The model we use in this paper follows the morphological parametrization of the Unification Scheme Antonucci1993 and observational constraints on the composition and geometry of NGC 4151.

We fix its outer radius to 2 pc according to the most recent constraints obtained by Schnulle2015 on NGC 4151 using dust reverberation techniques. The inner radius of the torus and its half-opening angle (with respect to the polar axis) are left to vary according to the desired X-ray flux or . Finally, along the pole, highly ionized winds carry out momentum and energy in the form of a double-conical region extending up to 400 pc Das2005 . Its half-opening angle (from the polar axis) is set to 33 ^ , as suggested by Das2005 based on long-slit spectroscopy and kinematic modeling of NGC 4151.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC4151 as a long-monitored periodic AGN candidate, with variability over decades, matching light and radial-velocity periodicity, and possible supermassive binary black-hole interpretation.

quotes.quote: There are light curves showing variability in over 100 years of observations in some objects, for example NGC4151 ), 3c273 ( ), with long variability timescale of more than one decade.

Until now there are only few candidates with the detection of the same periodicity in both light and radial velocity curves: e. g., NGC 4151 (Bon et al. 2012) and NGC 5548 (Li et al 2016, Bon et al. 2016). Both candidates have been monitored for very long time intervals, with probably the longest and best sampled data series of spectra and light curves, spanning over 43 years for NGC5548 and over 25 yr for NGC4151.
Some of these periodic AGN are recognized as SMBBH candidates, like NGC4151 , OJ287 , PG1302-102 .

sources.summary: The paper fits the host with a classical bulge and disk, while warning that dust lanes and small field of view make the decomposition uncertain.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 (Fig. 11.37) --- \ \ The host galaxy is fit with a classical bulge ( 13.3 mag; n=4 ) and a disk ( n=1 ). Owing to the prominent dust lanes and the small FoV of the HRC image, the fitting results may be uncertain. Based on fitting the same \ image, Bentz et al. (2009) find a brighter bulge ( 12 mag) with a much higher B/T ( 0.76 ). Using the ground-based images, Virani et al. (2000) find B/T 0.44 .

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4151 as the key polarized reverberation-mapping case, where a flux lag constrained the unresolved equatorial scattering region of its AGN.

quotes.quote: By comparing the amplitude of variability of the polarized flux to the amplitude of variability of the total flux in a blue passband with effective wavelength of 3600  , Gaskell2012 found that the polarized flux of the Seyfert-1 NGC 4151 follows the total flux with a lag of 8 3 days. This, together with the orientation of the position angle of the polarized flux, enabled to constrain the effective size of the unresolved equatorial scattering region of the AGN, most probably composed of electrons. This is the first and unique example so far illustrating the importance of polarized reverberation mapping in resolving the innermost structure of AGN. Achieving those observations is time consuming and required about seven years of monitoring the total and polarized fluxes of NGC 4151.

sources.summary: The paper discusses two suggested UFOs in NGC 4151, checking FUSE observations for predicted H I Ly counterparts and finding only non-repeatable flux undulations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 contains two suggested UFOs, one at v_ out = -0.105 Tombesi10 , and one at v_ out = -0.0555 Gofford13 . Both have predicted locations for H 1 Ly lying in the FUSE band, for which three separate observations are available. The wavelength regions spanned by the predicted wavelengths of a Ly counterpart contain several narrow absorption features that correspond to foreground interstellar lines, as well as a strong intrinsic absorption line from the metastable levels of C 3 * 1176. Although there are some large-scale undulations in flux in each of these regions, they are not present in all observations nor in both detector segments, nor do they rise to the level of significance of comparable features seen in the FUSE spectra of Mrk 79.

sources.summary: The paper uses Swift UVW2 monitoring of NGC 4151 in reverberation-mapping simulations, specifying its dense cadence and assigning a DRW variability model.

quotes.quote: We use the Swift UVW2-band lightcurves for the other three AGNs. Swift monitored NGC 4151 in 2016 from February 20 to April 29, yielding a lightcurve that consists of 319 visits with nearly five visits per day Edelson2017 . The lightcurve of NGC 4593 contains 148 epochs with a cadence of about 96 minutes from July 13 to July 18 in 2016 and a cadence of about 192 minutes in the following 16.2 days McHardy2018 . The observations of Mrk 509 span from March 17 to December 15 in 2017 with 257 epochs separated by about one day Edelson2019 .

We adopted DRW models with the parameters ( _ DRW , _ DRW ) = (17.38,125) , (0.72,136) , (0.49,86) , (0.77,146) for NGC 5548, NGC 4151, NGC 4593 and Mrk 509, respectively, where _ DRW is in units of days and _ DRW is in the same flux units as the observed lightcurves. We created 200 realizations of the simulated continuum for each object.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4151 as a Seyfert 1 where Chandra spectra show an asymmetrically broadened narrow Fe K line and mismatched inner and outer disk inclinations.

quotes.quote: Moreover, recent advances using 3rd-order spectra from Chandra HETG find that the narrow Fe K line in one Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 4151 is, in fact, asymmetrically broadened, suggestive of Doppler shifts and weak gravitational effects miller18 . This allows for determination of both the radius and inclination of the narrow Fe K emitting region. In this one tantalizing case, the outer and inner disk inclinations do not match, which might suggest a change in the vertical scale height of the disk at large radii due to radiation pressure. Interestingly, similar interpretations of changes in vertical disk structure have also been suggested gardner17 to explain intensive disk reverberation mapping of NGC 4151 edelson17 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4151 as a changing-look Seyfert whose continuum reconstructions remain inconclusive and whose [O III] flux stayed stable despite long-term continuum and spectral-type changes.

quotes.quote: Early hypothesis to solve this type 1/type 2 dichotomy were based on changing look Seyfert nuclei such as NGC 4151 1984MNRAS.211P..33P , where the appearance and disappearance of the broad lines and the blue featureless continuum were explained as on/off switches of the nuclear source.

Using this argument, 1999ApJ...512..204A,2000ApJ...536..710A combined all the UV, optical, and IR lines from the spectra of NGC 4151, Circinus, and NGC 1068 to derive the best-fitting photo-ionising continuum that reproduces the observed line fluxes in each case. The results are consistent with ionisation by a disc emission in Circinus, while the cases of NGC 1068 and NGC 4151 are still inconclusive due to the presence of abrupt absorption features in the reconstructed continuum.

This is the case of NGC 4151, where the flux of the [ O iii ] line at 5007 \ has been stable for decades despite the continuum variability and spectral type transitions 1983ApJ...271..564A,1997A&A...324..904M .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4151 lightcurves and finds temperature-map structures that remain stable over smoothing, especially alternating hot and cold patches across the disc.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 In Figure fig:ngc4151_panel , we show the temperature maps constructed using N_t = 100 and ranging from 1 to 1000. In Figure fig:ngc4151_lc , we show the observed lightcurves and the model lightcurves constructed using =10 . Compared to the other AGNs, the main structures appear relatively stable across the full range of smoothing . The most prominent feature of the map, alternating hot and cold patches between u=10 and u=100 , appears even with high degrees of smoothing ( = 100 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4151 as a cleaner comparison source, describing variable absorption, Swift BAT monitoring, a radial transition, and truncated optically thick material.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 gave a cleaner comparison as although this also shows strongly variable absorption, it is bright enough to be monitored by the Swift BAT instrument, sensitive to the higher energy 20-50 keV flux which is unaffected by the absorption. This showed clearly that there was a radial transition in the flow, with no reverberating material within 500-1000R_g Mahmoud20 . Such a hole in the optically thick material is also now consistent with the X-ray iron line profile Miller18 and its reverberation Zoghbi19 , despite previous claims to the contrary (e.g. Keck15, Zoghbi12, Cackett14 ). The new SED models had indeed predicted truncation of the optically thick disc material, though on somewhat smaller size scales of 50-100R_g .

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 4151 as one of two nearby NGC 1068-like galaxies whose expected neutrino luminosity and IceCube excess made it potentially detectable.

quotes.quote: There, a list of nearby galaxies similar to NGC 1068 was constructed and their expected neutrino luminosities were estimated. Taking into account these luminosity estimates and positions in the sky, it was found that the current IceCube's sensitivity should be sufficient to detect neutrino signal only from two more galaxies (besides NGC 1068 itself), NGC 4151 and NGC 3079. The authors analyzed a 10-year public catalog of IceCube events and found excesses of neutrinos from these sources with statistical significances of 3.0 and 3.9 , correspondingly. Note that, according to , both these sources also have a soft spectrum, though the values of power-law indices are not given in the paper.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4151 as the previous asymmetric narrow Fe K case, with Chandra spectroscopy and reverberation mapping placing the line-emitting region near the inner optical/X-ray BLR.

quotes.quote: Using Chandra grating spectra, miller2018_NGC4151 found that the narrow Fe K line in NGC 4151 is asymmetric, with the data suggesting that the emission could originate in a region as small as 50–130 _g during high ﬂux intervals The gravitational radius _g is a characteristic length scale associated with black holes and represents the radius at which the gravitational pull of a black hole becomes significant compared to other forces. The formula for calculating _g is: r_g = GM/^2 . . On a parallel front, zoghbi2019_NGC4151 measured the time delay between the narrow Fe K line and the hard X-ray continuum in NGC 4151 to constrain the location of the narrow Fe K region--a technique called reverberation mapping--and found a time delay of 3.4 ^ +2.5 _ 0.8 days. bentz2006 conducted a similar reverberation mapping study 13 years prior, but of the optical BLR in NGC 4151, and found a time delay of 6.6 ^ +1.1 _ 0.8 days between the optical H line and the continuum. These results indicate that the narrow Fe K line in NGC 4151 is primarily excited in either the innermost part of the optical broad line region (BLR), the X-ray BLR, or closer.

sources.summary: The atlas treats NGC 4151 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 . NGC 4151 is probably the best-studied Seyfert 1 galaxy in the literature, to which we owe much of our understanding about the AGN phenomenon. Its nuclear continuum and BLR emission are highly variable see, e.g., mao91,kas96 , during its low-luminosity state may display characteristics of a Seyfert 2 nucleus pp84 . Observations of this source span the full electromagnetic spectrum, including the NIR. Simultaneous JHK spectroscopy was previously reported by th095 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4151 as a strongly absorbed bright Seyfert with contemporaneous OSSE data, dual-absorber modeling, and average fitted reflection/spectral-slope values.

quotes.quote: Finally, we include an observation of NGC 4151 contemporaneously by both and the OSSE detector on board of CGRO , which latter data allow a determination of the strength of reflection in this strongly-absorbed bright Seyfert (Zdziarski, Johnson Magdziarz 1996).

In the case of NGC 4151, we assume i=65 and fit the /OSSE data with a dual absorber model (Zdziarski et al. \ 1996) and a thermal-Comptonization continuum model (Misra et al., in preparation). The above procedure gives us R and with their error contours.
We also see that NGC 4151 and 4U 0241+61 show rather average values of and R .

sources.summary: The catalogue identifies NGC 4151 as a Seyfert 1.5 extended source and reports a clear 0.5-mag weakening of its central optical region between 2003 and 2007.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 is an extended source, classified as Seyfert 1.5, much larger than the OMC aperture. We detected a clear weakening of its central, dominating region by around 0.5 mag (60 in flux) from 11.15 mag to 11.65 mag between May 2003 and January 2007. Since the optical photometry is contaminated to some extent by bulge stellar light, the variation in the optical emission from the nucleus itself might have been significantly larger.

sources.summary: NGC 4151 shows changing iron-line variability: one observation has a distinctive red-side component and relativistic-looking excess, while another flat rms spectrum argues for invariant reflection.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151(2) is unique in displaying a significantly variable narrow component to the red side of the line, greatly in excess of the time averaged profile.
Like MCG-6-30-15(1), NGC 4151(2) shows evidence for excess variability between 5.0-6.2 keV, whereas NGC 4151(3) is flat.

Interestingly, N07 found that the third observation, NGC 4151(3) strongly requires a broad and relativistic iron line but the first two observations of this object, while being consistent with a broad line, did not need one.

sources.summary: The paper reports iron-K reverberation in NGC 4151, finding 2000 s X-ray delays and a broad lag profile consistent with reflection from the inner accretion disk.

quotes.quote: Using XMM-Newton observations of NGC 4151, we find delays of order 2000 s on time-scales of 10^5 s between the 5-6 keV band and 2-3 and 7-8 keV bands, with a broad lag profile resembling a relativistically-broadened iron line. The peak of the lag spectra shifts to lower energies at higher frequencies, consistent with the red wing of the line being emitted at smaller radii, as expected from reflection off the inner accretion disk. This is a first detection of a broad iron line using timing studies.

sources.summary: NGC 4151 is used as a type 1 AGN example where broad-line smoothness implies many BLR clouds, high cloud density, and interferometric consistency with Seyfert 2 tori.

quotes.quote: High quality observations of the profile of optical broad emission lines suggest that the minimum number of clouds needed to reproduce the observed smoothness is quite high: one of the most striking examples is that of NGC 4151, with an estimated number of at least 10 ^8 , based on high S/N Keck spectra arav98 . Considering that the estimated black hole mass of NGC 4151 is of the order of 10 ^7 M _ , and that the distance of BLR clouds is of the order of 10 ^4 gravitational radii, a huge cloud density is obtained, with a nearly complete occupation of the available volume.
The first observation carried on a type 1 object, NGC 4151, led to results in agreement with the previous ones on Seyfert 2s bur09 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4151 as a nearby Type 1 Seyfert where an X-ray continuum and broad iron-line lag could probe inner-disk illumination geometry.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 : A nearby Type-1 Seyfert with relatively large absorption column weaver:94 . Recent observations suggest a time lag between the continuum and a broad iron line, with the continuum leading by  2000 s zoghbi:12 . Combined with X-ray polarization, this promises to be a powerful probe of the illumination geometry of the inner disk.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4151 as a Seyfert galaxy whose light-curve, radial-velocity, and H-line variations can be explained by an eccentric sub-parsec supermassive binary black hole.

quotes.quote: Recently, Bon ( ) has shown that a SMBBHs exists in the core of Seyfert galaxy NGC 4151. The periodic variations in the light curves and the radial velocity curves of NGC 4151 can be accounted for by an eccentric ( e=0.42 ), sub-parsec Keplerian orbit with a 15.9 year period. The periodic variations in the observed H_ line shape and flux are well explained in the model of the shock which is generated by the supersonic motion of the SMBBHs through the surrounding medium, rather than the periodic disk accretion.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4151's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 is a nearly face-on barred spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 13.3 1 Mpc mundell_nuclear_2003 with a Sy 1.5 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . This galaxy is one of the original six Seyfert galaxies seyfert_nuclear_1943 and host the flux-brightest, nearest and most variable type I AGN. Therefore, it belongs to the best-studied AGN at all wavelengths (see ulrich_active_2000 for a review).

sources.summary: The paper simulates NGC 4151 as a promising ASTRO-H target, using its 2002 HETG flux state and absorber model with added high-ionization gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 We simulated the spectrum of NGC 4151 using the same flux condition of the 2002 HETG spectrum kraemer05 . We used their absorber model (adopting the xabs model fo SPEX for the absorbers) and added two slab components for the higher ionization gas. See Table  tab:4151sim for the parameters and Figure  fig:ngc4151 for the simulated spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4151 as a key Seyfert target, finds a marginal gamma-ray signal complicated by nearby source S, and derives hot-flow constraints.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 At the position of NGC 4151 we found a signal with the test-statistic significance TS 17 for E=0.2-6 GeV, see Fig. \ fig:tsmap d.

The residual seen in the TS map for this data set, Fig. \ fig:tsmap (a), can be fully compensated for by adding the point source at the position of NGC 4151 (compare with panel b), and gtlike gives TS 8 , = 2.7 0.3 and F_ >0.1 GeV = (1.5 0.6) 10^ 9 for this source.

The presence of a variable source S results in an ambiguity for the UL on the ray flux from NGC 4151, especially below 1 GeV, where the point spread function of LAT is much higher than the angular separation between S and NGC 4151.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4151 as a Seyfert 1 where X-ray, UV, and optical variability were strongly correlated without detectable lags, supporting UV reprocessing by primary X-rays.

quotes.quote: A multiwavelength campaign undertaken by Edelson96 observed a strong correlation between the X-ray, UV and optical variability in the Seyfert 1 (Sy 1) galaxy NGC 4151, with no detectable lags. The UV observations were taken with a sampling interval of 0.05 d while the X-ray observations were taken twice per day. The obtained results suggest that UV emission in the source is produced by the reprocessing of primary X-rays.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4151 as a Seyfert galaxy whose spatially resolved NLR kinematics and integrated spectra were reproduced with modified biconical outflow and dust-plane models.

quotes.quote: For example, Das:2005ff modeled the NLR kinematics of a Seyfert galaxy NGC 4151, which are spatially resolved based on the HST/STIS data, by modifying the radial velocity profile. Later, StorchiBergmann:2010fg modified the bicone model by considering weak emission from the steeply inclined wall of the cone and a constant radial velocity, in order to reproduce the PV diagram of NGC 4151 measured based on the integral-field spectroscopy obtained with the Gemini/NIFS .

2010ApJ...708..419C combined the biconical outflow model with a thin dust plane, which can partly obscure the outflows from the line-of-sight (LOS), reproducing the blueshifted [ ] of NGC 1068 and NGC 4151 in their spatially integrated spectra.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC4151 as an AGN with hybrid-corona modeling constraints: OSSE limited its nonthermal contribution below 15 percent, and INTEGRAL spectra fit thermal models to 200 keV while varying by state.

quotes.quote: Comparison of hybrid models with AGN spectra has been carried out on NGC4151 Zdziarski1996,Johnson1997 , using OSSE data from the Compton Gamma-Ray Observatory to conclude that the nonthermal contribution in NGC4151 is less than 15 per cent.

More recent work on NGC4151 using INTEGRAL data Lubinski2010 shows that thermal models fit the spectra, which extend up to 200 keV, in its bright state and that the source appears harder in its low state. There is no new information on any possible hard tail or annhilation feature; OSSE results remain the most sensitive at those energies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4151 as a type-1 Seyfert with a good soft X-ray/[O III] match; continuum subtraction has little effect because [O III] dominates the filter.

quotes.quote: This is also the case in the type-1 Seyfert NGC 4151, but not in NGC 1365, both presenting a good soft X-ray/ [ O iii ] match.

NGC 4151 : A typical example of drastically change from continuum to [ O iii ] emission resembling the soft X-ray morphology. As shown in Fig.  fig:subs_ngc4151_ngc4388 the difference from [ O iii ] +continuum to pure [ O iii ] is minimum.

sources.summary: The monitoring review identifies NGC 4151 as a strongly variable Seyfert 1.5 and calculates its SMBH mass from broad-line reverberation properties.

quotes.quote: Here we present some interesting findings of the long-term (more than a decade) optical monitoring campaign of a sample of seven type 1 AGN of different spectral types: Seyfert 1 galaxies (NGC 5548, NGC 4151, NGC 7469), Narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy - NLSy 1 (Ark 564), double-peaked line radio loud (3C 390.3) and radio quiet (Arp 102B) galaxy, and a luminous quasar (E1821+643), a supermassive binary black hole candidate.

In case of NGC 5548 and NGC 4151 the mass was not derived in the original monitoring campaign, so we calculated in this work their SMBH masses, using the equation M_ BH =f R_ BLR V^2_ FWHM /G , where V_ FWHM is the orbital velocity estimated from the width of the variable part of H (taken from references given in Table 1), and f is the virial factor taken to be 5.5 On04 .

From Table 1 and Figure fig:1 , we can see that: NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 are strongly variable Seyfert 1.5, NGC 7469 is also a Seyfert 1 but with lower variability in line fluxes and profiles, Arp 102B and 3C 390.3 have broad double-peaked lines, Ark 564 is a NLSy1, and E1821+643 is a quasar with strong red-asymmetry in line profiles, but almost no variability.

sources.summary: The paper compares its wind-scattering model to NGC 4151, where frequency-dependent iron-line lag shifts are consistent with wind material hundreds of gravitational radii out.

quotes.quote: Such shifts are seen in NGC 4151 zog12 , where at frequencies 2 10^ 5 Hz there is a distinct feature around the iron line energy, which becomes much broader, i.e., much less distinct at higher frequencies (see Figs. \ 2 and 7 of zog12 ).

The line response is strongly suppressed on frequencies smaller than /R , so the data from NGC 4151 are then consistent with being from a wind at a few 100's of R_g rather than 5 R_g as modelled by cac14 .
The correlation of line of sight velocity in the wind and lag time leads to shifts in the observed energy of the line as a function of frequency, similar to those seen in NGC 4151 zog12 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4151 shows UV spectral hardening in SWP and LWP bands, with the spectral index becoming nearly constant at higher flux levels.

quotes.quote: This is seen in the sources NGC 3783, NGC 4151, NGC 4593 and Fairall 9 in SWP. In LWP, this is evident in the sources NGC 4151 and Fairall 9.

For some sources in our sample such as NGC 3783, NGC 4151, NGC 4593 and Fairall 9, we in fact observed a constancy of the spectral index with increasing flux, however, only beyond certain flux levels in them.

sources.summary: The paper includes special emission features for NGC 4151 and attributes its anomalously high column density to intermittent obscuration rather than the modeled outflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 included the following emission features as well: 7.13 \ (Si K ), 9.17 \ (Mg ^ +10 ), 9.31 \ (Mg ^ +10 f ), 12.13 \ (Ne ^ +9 Ly ), 16.01 \ (O ^ +7 Ly ), 16.8 \ (O ^ +6 RRC).

The anomalously high \ in NGC 4151 is due to its intermittent obscuration (see Section sec:intro ). Its high N_ H ( _i) > 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 values at = -1 and = 1 , which none of the other AGNs features (see Table tab:columns ) likely have little to do with the outflow.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4151 recurrence plots as evolving from dense blocks to sparse diagonal structures and finds deterministic non-linearity with non-stationarity tests.

quotes.quote: Some of the RPs also demonstrate changes in the texture, or small-scale patterns, over time. This is most evident in NGC 4151 in which we see dense, block-like patterns in the RP at early times that slowly evolve into larger and more sparse diagonally-oriented structures. The diagonal structures in this source also appear to not be parallel to the main diagonal of the RP, which indicates a slow change in the underlying dynamics.

We find that four sources contain evidence for deterministic non-linearity according to a standard surrogate data test (simple nonlinear prediction error): ESO 506-027, NGC 2992, NGC 4151, and NGC 5548, all of which were identified as non-stationary from the ADF and KPSS non-stationarity tests.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4151 as a barred Seyfert 1, measuring stellar mass, rotation consistency, and compact NLR outflow velocities and extents.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 is a barred Seyfert (De Vaucouleurs classification: (R')SAB(rs)ab ) galaxy with a type 1 AGN as indicated by broad Balmer emission (including and ) from its nucleus Shapovalova2010 .
For NGC 4151, we measure a total stellar mass of 2.5 10 ^ 10 M_ , which is close to the total stellar mass ( 2.2 10 ^ 10 M_ ) provided in .
NGC 4151: We find the maximum extent of the outflows is 300 pc from the nucleus, with true velocities ranging from 200--800 for the majority of the knots, and a few knots ranging up to 1200 .

sources.summary: NGC 4151 is a sample AGN whose JAVELIN lag disagrees with ICCF-Cut; the paper attributes the suspicious long-lag behavior partly to a short observing baseline.

quotes.quote: The lags given by the Pmap model are similar to that of ICCF-Cut for NGC 5548, NGC 2617, Mrk 142 and Mrk 509, while not consistent for NGC 4593 and NGC 4151.

JAVELIN lag _ jav of NGC 4151 is much larger than the ICCF-Cut result, while the lag distribution of NGC 4593 has multiple strong peaks, which makes it suspicious to be a true estimation, even though the JAVELIN lag could reach consistency within the error bar.
The monitoring duration is 69.3 days for NGC 4151 and 22.6 days for NGC 4593 2019ApJ...870..123E while the duration of other targets is more than 100 days.

sources.summary: The dissertation summarizes IXPE results for NGC 4151, noting coronal-geometry constraints, complications from distant reflection, and radio-aligned X-ray polarization.

quotes.quote: Reprocessing in distant equatorial covering regions was also considered in the IXPE analysis of the (unobscured) AGN NGC 4151 Gianolli2023 .

Similar geometrical conclusions for the corona were derived for the December 2022 observations of NGC 4151 4.9 1.1 in 2--8 keV at a 68 confidence level, Gianolli2023 , where the reflection from distant parsec-scale components, however, complicated the spectropolarimetric disentangling. The detected X-ray polarisation in NGC 4151 was aligned with the direction of extended radio emission Harrison1986,Ulvestad1998 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4151 as its Seyfert-galaxy accretion-flow case, noting that thermal synchrotron models underpredict its luminosity and adopting specific source parameters.

quotes.quote: They correspond to the models of thermal CS emission from a hot accretion disc with magnetic field in equipartition with the gas internal energy (dominated by hot ions) of WZ00 for the Seyfert galaxy NGC 4151 and two BHBs in their hard states, GX 339--4 and Cyg X-1. The CS luminosities predicted by the thermal model (fig. \ 8a in WZ00) were orders of magnitude below the ones observed from NGC 4151 and GX 339--4, and less by a factor of 3 for Cyg X-1. For NGC 4151, we assume (see WZ00 for details and references) R=1.2 10^ 14 cm, B=3.8 10^3 G, =0.85 , =0.1 , p=4 , _ f =100 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4151 as an AGN whose faint soft X-ray component lacks variability, matches thermal gas near narrow-line clouds, and is detectable despite intrinsic absorption.

quotes.quote: For instance, Pounds et al. (1986) showed the existence of a soft X-ray spectral component, distinct from the main (nuclear) X-ray emission of NGC 4151 by a lack of variability. This component is shown to be consistent with thermal emission from a hot, confining medium in the region of the narrow emission-line clouds. Perhaps NGC 4151 is an exception to the general rule that soft X-ray emission from AGNs is variable, because most of its soft X-ray flux is absorbed by a high intrinsic column density, so that its faint, diffuse X-ray flux can be detected.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC4151 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy whose dust-torus inner radius, measured from a V-to-K reverberation lag, roughly matches the predicted dust-evaporation radius.

quotes.quote: The intense monitoring observations were carried out on the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC4151, and a lag time of 48^ +2 _ 3 days between the V and K light curves was measured. The inner radius of the dust torus in NGC4151 is 0.04 pc corresponding to this measured time lag. For this source, the predicted evaporation radius R(T_ evap ) 0.015 pc (Netzer Laor, 1996; L_ bol 6.5 10^ 36 W, given by Kaspi et al., 2000), which is roughly consistent with the inner radius of the dust torus 0.04 pc measured by thermal dust reverberation method m04 .

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 4151 has available BLR inclination estimates but no clear consensus, with published values ranging from about 12 degrees to as high as 70 degrees.

quotes.quote: Of the seven Population 2 objects in the Onken sample, estimates are available for only two AGNs, 3C 390.3 and NGC 4151.

In the case of NGC 4151, the deduced inclination ranges from 12 - 21 ^ (Boksenberg et al. \ 1995) and 18-- 23 ^ (Winge et al. \ 1999), to as high as 40 ^ (Pedlar et al. \ 1998) or even 70^ (Kaiser et al. \ 2000). There is thus no clear consensus on the inclination of this system.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4151 as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy with flat power law, strong absorption, soft excess, broad Fe K line, and modeled neutral absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151 This Seyfert 1.5 galaxy NGC 4151 has long been known that it appears to have a flat power law ( 1.3 - 1.7 ), a substantial column density of absorbing gas (N _ H 10 ^ 23 cm ^ 2 ), a strong soft excess at energies below 2 keV, and the broad iron K emission line. We take the result of Model 2 in Schurch2003 , who modeled the spectrum with two absorption components, a partially photoionized absorber and a neutral absorber, and a neutral reflection continuum. The column density of the neutral absorber is listed in Table table:2-1 and the absorption correction has been made with their Model 2.

gmw_00000033.name: NGC 4993
gmw_00000033.overview: NGC 4993 is a nearby lenticular [arXiv:1710.09590, arXiv:1710.05452] or elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1710.05851, arXiv:1810.10674] that hosted the gravitational-wave event GW170817 [arXiv:1710.09590, arXiv:1802.04379]. The galaxy features an old stellar population [arXiv:1809.03525, arXiv:1710.05444], very low star formation [arXiv:1809.03525, arXiv:1903.00083], and shell and dust structures indicating a recent minor merger [arXiv:1801.01493, arXiv:1710.06748].

sources.summary: NGC 4993 is described as the nearby S0 host of GW 170817A with weak LLAGN activity; the paper models its multiwaveband SED using an ADAF-jet scenario.

quotes.quote: NGC 4993, as a host galaxy of the electromagnetic counterpart of the first gravitational-wave detection of a binary neutron-star merger, was observed by many powerful telescopes from radio to ray waveband. The weak nuclear activities of NGC 4993 suggest that it is a low-luminosity active galactic nuclear (LLAGN). We build the multi-waveband spectral energy distributions (SEDs) of NGC 4993 from literatures.

NGC 4993 is a nearby S0 galaxy, which is the host galaxy of the EM counterpart for the GW 170817A ab17 . It has a redshift of z=0.009873 , corresponding to a distance of 40 Mpc lev17 . The weak [ OIII ] , [ NII ] , [ SII ] emission lines are presented in nucleus, and the relatively high ratio of NII 6583/H is suggestive of a LLAGNs rather than star formation may be also driven by some hot post-AGB stars or shocks, e.g., lev17 .

We model the multi-waveband SEDs of NGC 4993 with an advection-dominated accretion flow (ADAF)-jet model. We find that the high-frequency steep radio emission at millimeter waveband is consistent with the prediction of the ADAF, while the low-frequency flat radio spectrum is better fitted by the jet.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4993 as the galaxy hosting GW170817 and uses its known direction and distance to constrain Super-Kamiokande neutrino fluence and radiated energy.

quotes.quote: These observations led to the conclusion that the merger happened in galaxy NGC4993 and was followed by a short gamma-ray burst and a kilonova/macronova . High-energy neutrino signals associated with the merger were also searched for by the ANTARES, IceCube, and Pierre Auger Observatories. It was concluded that no significant neutrino signal was observed . We report the results of a search for neutrinos in Super-Kamiokande (SK) associated with this gravitational wave signal produced by the binary neutron star merger in NGC4993.

In 60.4 of the following 14 days, NGC4993 is within the sensitive half. Since the direction of NGC4993 is well known , for UPMU data, for which the angular resolution is better than for the other two samples, we concentrated on a 5^ cone around NGC4993 for the event search in the following 14 days. This method was previously used in SK to search for neutrino signals associated with astrophysical objects . The 5^ constraint was not used for the 500-s search in Table event_table because no event was observed in all sky during this window, and unlike the 14-day-window case, the zenith angle change of NGC4993 in 500-s can be ignored.

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC 4993 as GW170817's early-type host, emphasizing its old stellar population, very low star formation, and implications for long neutron-star merger delay times.

quotes.quote: This first NS-NS merger detection occurred in NGC 4993, an early-type galaxy ( GW170817-EM,2017Sci...358.1556C ). Analysis of the properties of this galaxy revealed a current stellar mass of about 0.3-1.4 10^ 11 M _ and a star formation rate (if any) below 10 ^ 2 M _ yr ^ 1 ( 2017ApJ...848L..22B,2017ApJ...849L..16I,2017ApJ...848L..28L,2017ApJ...848L..30P ), which is significantly lower compared to what is observed in late-type galaxies having similar stellar masses (see Figure 4 in 2016A&A...590A..44G ). According to , an upper limit of only 1 of the stellar mass could originate from young stars.

By convolving the star formation history of NGC 4993 with a DTD function in the form of t^ 1 , calculated the probability of the merger timescale to be between 6.8 and 13.6 Gyr, with 90 confidence. According to population synthesis models, the DTD function of NS-NS mergers does extend up to 10 Gyr, but most of the merger events are likely to occur within the first Gyr following the formation of the progenitor stars (e.g., 2018MNRAS.474.2937C ). Therefore, according to , detecting the first NS-NS merger event in an early-type galaxy was unlikely, although not impossible. The low probability of this event is also discussed in . With only one NS-NS gravitational wave detection, conclusions are limited, but the host galaxy of GW170817 does inform us that it is at least possible for NS-NS mergers to have Gyr-long coalescence timescales.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4993 as the elliptical host of the GW170817 optical counterpart, with studied morphology, old stellar population, dust-lane evidence, and a redshift useful for Hubble-constant estimates.

quotes.quote: About 10.9 hours later, optical counterpart was discovered by Swope supernova survey in an elliptical galaxy NGC4993.

The host galaxy NGC 4993 has been also studied in detail . Based on the deep image obtained by stacking large number of images taken during the followup observation campaign at many different wavelengths, morphological, structural and photometric properties have been analyzed. The surface brightness profile of NGC 4993 found to follow those typical elliptical galaxies.

There is The spectral energy distribution of NGC 4993 was used to probe the star formation history. By fitting the observed spectral energy distribution covering from 1.5 nm to 20 m to the population synthesis models, mean stellar age is estimated to be greater than 3 Gyrs. This means that the neutron star binary was formed more than 3 Gyrs ago, indicating that the neutron star binary may have formed no later than 3 Gyrs ago. The deep Hubble Space Telescope image in 606 nm for the inner parts of the galaxy shows trace of dust lane, implying the possible minor merger with a gas rich galaxy recently, although there is no evidence for recent star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4993's Spitzer/IRAC host-galaxy light, removing a Sersic profile and noting tidal-shell residuals while measuring infrared structural parameters used for transient photometry.

quotes.quote: Archival imaging of NGC 4993 also exists from 2014 (PID 10043, PI K. Sheth, we stacked 2014-10-12 and 2014-09-12 data). Data was reduced and mosaiced by the IRAC pipeline. The IRAC point-spread function is complex: although its core is compact, significant light is scattered far from the position of an object in a complex, asymmetric pattern. The fixed detector/optical diffraction patterns in the IRAC PSF profiles complicates identification of sources in the vicinity of the bright galaxy core since they cannot be easily matched and subtracted between observations taken at different position angles. Specifically, the position angles were 106 deg for Epoch 1, 114 deg for Epoch 2, 65 deg for Epoch 3 and 108 deg for the archival observation. Given the complexity of the underlying galaxy background and the mismatched position angles, we undertook several independent data analysis methods that facilitated multiple consistency checks. We first describe our preferred method that yielded the highest Signal to Noise (S/N) detections presented in Table tab:data . To remove the flux from the bright galaxy, we employ the software package GALFIT Peng2010 , interfaced via a custom python wrapper for galaxy subtraction Perley2016 .

We used the first reference observation to fit a Sersic model for the host galaxy profile (simultaneously fitting the 20 brightest stars within the fitting box). The Point Response Function (PRF) files for both Spitzer/IRAC bands were downloaded from the Spitzer website and used as the PSFs, and our fit is restricted to a 58x58 arcsec box around the galaxy centroid. A reasonable fit is obtained for this model and most of the galaxy light and foreground starlight is effectively removed (Figure fig:sub ). We see some asymmetric residuals which visually match the structure of the tidal 'shells' visible in HST imaging of the field as well as some residuals from much fainter foreground stars and background galaxies.

At 4.5 m, We measure a Sersic index of 4.24, a half-light radius of 10.74 arcseconds, an integrated magnitude of 12.29 (AB), and an axis ratio of 0.849. At 3.6 m, we measure a Sersic index of 4.65, a half-light radius of 12.38 arcseconds, axis ratio of 0.838 and integrated magnitude of 11.65 AB.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4993 as GW170817's old host galaxy, emphasizing its early star-formation history, low current star formation, shell/dust structures, and minor recent star formation.

quotes.quote: In this galaxy, NGC 4993, star formation peaked 11 Gyr ago (at level 10 ) and then was exponentially declining, leading to a very low current star formation rate ( 0.01 ). The total mass formed in stars was estimated at 7.9 10^ 10 . In particular, the 50 of stars formed by 11.2 Gyr ago, while 90 of stars formed by 6.8 Gyr ago   Blanchard2017 . Stars in NGC 4993 appear to have near solar chemical composition  Blanchard2017,Troja2017,Palmese2017 . It was reported that the central parts of NGC 4993 appear to have shell and dust structures that may be indicative of a recent minor galaxy merger or mergers.

Finally, present detailed spectral, photometric and image analysis to calculate the star formation history in NGC 4993. In fact, this analysis reveals some extra component in the recent star formation history indicative of a minor merger (see the flattening at t=0.1 - 1 Gyr ago in their exponentially declining SFR; their Fig.3 left-bottom panel). However, this recent episode provides only a small fraction of the overall stellar mass in NGC 4993.

sources.summary: The paper assesses GW170817 host NGC 4993, using its high stellar mass, low star formation, and group environment to infer longer binary-neutron-star delay times.

quotes.quote: Following the GW detection, electromagnetic searches of the GW localization volume succeeded in identifying its optical counterpart and associated host galaxy NGC 4993 Coulter:2017,GW170817:DES,GW170817:MMA . NGC 4993 has a stellar mass of 0.3 - 1.2 10^ 11 M_ and an average star formation rate over a Gyr of 10^ 0.5 M_ yr ^ 1 Artale:2019doq . Given its high stellar mass and low star-formation rate, it seems likely that NGC 4993 is drawn from a population that traces stellar mass more closely than star-formation rate (see Fig. fig:dists ). Given this single host galaxy, we use its position on the M_ -sSFR plane to estimate the likelihood of different models.

Figure fig:NGC_4993 shows the probability of different time-delay distributions given the star formation rate and stellar mass of NGC 4993. We find that NGC 4993 prefers a time-delay distribution with a minimum delay time that is longer than 1 Gyr and a relatively steep slope. The right hand panel of Fig fig:NGC_4993 shows the probability of different delay times when we marginalize over with a flat prior in the range 4< <0 . The preference for intermediate delay times is a consequence of the fact that NGC 4993 has a somewhat low average sSFR given its stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 4993 as the S0 host of SSS17a/GW170817, measuring its de Vaucouleurs-like profile, effective radius, stellar mass, dust lanes, and transient offset.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of SSS17a is NGC 4993, an S0-type galaxy with prominent lanes of cosmic dust concentrated at its core and reaching out nearly to the projected position of SSS17a . We analyzed the surface brightness of NGC 4993 using a Hubble Space Telescope ( ) imaging, and found that it decreases with projected radius ( R ) and a scaling constant ( k ) as exp (-k R^ 1/4 ) . This profile is a de Vaucouleurs profile and is typical for S0-type galaxies. Assuming this profile, we find NGC 4993 has an effective radius R_ e = 3.3  kpc (17 ). Fitting stellar population models to 13-band photometry of NGC 4993 , we find a galactic stellar mass of (M/M_ ) = 10.49^ +0.08 _ 0.20 , where M is the stellar mass of NGC 4993 and M_ is the mass of the Sun.

SSS17a is offset 10.6 \ from the center of NGC 4993, or 2.0 kpc in projection. This corresponds to an offset of 0.6R_ e , which is small compared to the typical offsets seen in population studies of SGRBs .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4993 as the host of EM 170817, using its distance and host emission to interpret Swift and NuSTAR limits and possible diffuse X-ray or AGN emission.

quotes.quote: At 01:05 UT on 2017 August 18, reported the detection of a candidate EM counterpart, hereafter denoted `EM 170817', apparently associated with the galaxy NGC 4993 (distance 40 Mpc ). Shortly thereafter, several others groups reported detection of the source , sometimes with differing names (e.g. \ SSS17a, DLT17ck).

This is 10.2 ^ \ from the position of EM 170817, and 0.9 ^ \ from the position of NGC 4993, suggesting that the emission detected is from the host galaxy, rather than the electromagnetic counterpart. This interpretation was confirmed by Chandra observations . Taken with the detection of radio emission from the galaxy this may indicate an active galactic nucleus in NGC 4993; however the X-ray emission appears somewhat diffuse and is thus likely made up of multiple X-ray emitters in that galaxy.

The non-detection of NGC 4993 in the >3 keV band pass of NuSTAR suggests a relatively soft spectrum, given the detection by Swift XRT. Indeed, we find no evidence for an elevated count-rate at the location of NGC 4993.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4993 as the S0 host near the transient, adopts a 40 Mpc distance, and notes little host extinction despite dust lanes.

quotes.quote: The sky location of the transient was RA (2000)=13 :09:48.09, Dec. (2000)=- 23:22:53.3, approximately 10 ^ from the centre of the S0 galaxy NGC 4993 (Figure fig:field ). Contemporaneous observations made independently with several optical telescopes also revealed a new source at this location coulter17,GCN21530,valenti17 , which was designated \ (also referred to as SSS17a and DLT17ck).

Although there is some evidence for dust lanes in the galaxy, its early-type nature and the absence of host absorption lines (Section sec:spec ) suggests little dust extinction. Furthermore, the transient is located away from these obviously dusty regions (see . for details of host morphology and transient location). This is supported by the linear polarimetry of the transient, which shows very low levels of polarisation covino17 , implying a line of sight dust column in the host galaxy of E(B-V) 0.2 mag (assuming a Milky Way like relation between E(B-V) and linear polarisation). Thus we only correct the photometry for dust extinction in the Milky Way.
The distance to NGC 4993 is not well established hjorth17 . The heliocentric velocity is 2930 km s ^ 1 z 0.0098 ; levan17 , and here we take the distance to be d=40 Mpc (distance modulus =33.01 ).

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 4993 as the slightly disturbed elliptical host of GW170817, deriving old stellar populations, weak star formation, merger features, and a fundamental-plane distance.

quotes.quote: We analyze the multi-wavelength data to characterize the host galaxy property and its distance to examine if the properties of NGC 4993 are consistent with this picture. Our analysis shows that NGC 4993 is a bulge-dominated galaxy with r_ eff 2-3 kpc and the S ersic index of n = 3-4 for the bulge component. The spectral energy distribution from 0.15 to 24 m indicates that this galaxy has no significant ongoing star formation, the mean stellar mass of (0.3 - 1.2) 10^ 11 , the mean stellar age greater than 3 Gyr, and the metallicity of about 20 to 100 of solar abundance. Optical images reveal dust lanes and extended features that suggest a past merging activity. Overall, NGC 4993 has characteristics of normal, but slightly disturbed elliptical galaxies.

Furthermore, we derive the distance to NGC 4993 with the fundamental plane relation using 17 parameter sets of 7 different filters and the central stellar velocity dispersion from literature, finding an angular diameter distance of 37.7 8.7 Mpc. NGC 4993 is similar to some host galaxies of short gamma-ray bursts but much different from those of long gamma-ray bursts, supporting the picture of GW170817 as a result of a merger of two NSs.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4993, the host galaxy of GW170817, to calculate radial peculiar velocity from gravitational-wave distance and electromagnetic redshift under different Hubble constants.

quotes.quote: Below, we calculate the peculiar velocity of the host galaxy of GW170817. By fitting the waveform signal of the GW170817, the distance of this event is 43.8 ^ +2.9 _ 6.9 Mpc . From the EM counterparts, the heliocentric redshift of host galaxy NGC 4993 is z=0.009783 , which corresponds to z = 0.01083 in the CMB frame. In order to derive the peculiar velocity from equation (1), the Hubble constant should be known.

The radial peculiar velocity is v_ pec, 276 km s^ 1 if H_0 from Planck is used. Using the error propagation formula, the uncertainty of v_ pec, is about 200 km s^ 1 . The radial peculiar velocity turns out to be v_ pec, 38 km s^ 1 for H_0 from SNe Ia .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC4993 as an early-type GW170817 host with shell structures and dust lanes from a recent dry minor merger, old stellar population, little ongoing star formation, and unusual sGRB-host properties.

quotes.quote: We present a study of NGC 4993, the host galaxy of the GW170817 gravitational wave event, the GRB170817A short gamma--ray burst (sGRB) and the AT2017gfo kilonova. We use Dark Energy Camera imaging, AAT spectra and publicly available data, relating our findings to binary neutron star (BNS) formation scenarios and merger delay timescales. NGC4993 is a nearby early--type galaxy, with i band S ersic index n=4.0 and low asymmetry ( A=0.04 0.01 ). These properties are unusual for sGRB hosts.

However, NGC4993 presents shell--like structures and dust lanes indicative of a recent galaxy merger, with the optical transient located close to a shell. We constrain the star formation history (SFH) of the galaxy assuming that the galaxy merger produced a star formation burst, but find little to no on--going star formation in either spatially--resolved broadband SED or spectral fitting. We use the best--fit SFH to estimate the BNS merger rate in this type of galaxy, as R_ NSM ^ gal = 5.7^ +0.57 _ 3.3 10^ 6 yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 4993 as an old, early-type, sub-solar-metallicity GW170817 host, estimates its cluster-related merger production, and notes shell/dust features that could indicate a recent galaxy merger.

quotes.quote: The host of GW170817, NGC 4993, is an early type galaxy with sub-solar metallicity and with virtually no current star formation. The peak of star formation was estimated at 10 Gyr (with exponential decay afterwards; ), or last estimated to take place t_ sf =3 - 7 Gyr ago Troja2017 .

The NGC 4993 galaxy has a stellar mass M_ * = 4.4 10^ 10 Blanchard2017 ; the corresponding dynamical mass can be calculated using Equation gllz . Hence, according to Eq. eq.gctot the initial mass in GC will be M_ gc,tot = 0.01 M_g = 3.3 10^8 M _ , while the GC initial average mass is given by Eq. eq.mgcave M_ gc,ave = 4.1 10^5 M _ , thus implying a number of GCs ending up in the galaxy nuclear star cluster N_ gc,NSC = f_c M_ gc,tot / M_ gc,ave 89 Using Eq. eq.gcmass-number we can calculate the total number of potential NS-NS mergers in this galaxy as N_ nsns = 0.001M_ gc,ave ^ 0.55 N_ gc,NSC = 108.

It is noted that NGC 4993 shows some shell structures and dust lanes that may be possibly indicative of a recent merger ( 200 - 400 Myr ago) with another smaller galaxy Palmese2017,Ebrova2018 . If there was a recent burst of massive star formation in NGC 4993 induced by galaxy merger, it is possible that GW170817 was formed in such an event and our analysis and conclusions do not hold.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4993 as an early-type shell galaxy and GW170817 host whose shells, dust, and low star formation point to a recent minor merger roughly 400 Myr ago.

quotes.quote: NGC 4993 is the shell galaxy host of the GRB170817A short gamma-ray burst and the GW170817 gravitational-wave event produced during a binary neutron star coalescence. The galaxy shows signs, including the stellar shells, that it recently accreted a smaller late-type galaxy. The accreted galaxy could have been the original host of the binary neutron star. We measure the positions of the stellar shells of NGC 4993 in an HST/ACS archival image and use them to constrain the time of the galactic merger. According to the analytical model of the evolution of the shell structure in the expected gravitational potential of NGC 4993, the galactic merger happened at least 200 Myr ago with a probable time roughly around 400 Myr and the estimates and higher than 600 Myr being improbable.

The HST image also uncovers a rich dust structure around the center of the galaxy. NGC 4993, as a big early-type galaxy, is expected to contain limited amount of gas, thus it is probable that the observed dust lanes could come from the accreted galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses HST globular-cluster candidates to estimate NGC 4993's distance, noting that it hosts GW170817/GRB 170817A and shows recent-merger dust lanes while having a large velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4993 hosts a binary neutron star merger emitting gravitational waves and electromagnetic waves, GW170817/GRB 170817A. The distance to this galaxy is not well established. We select the globular cluster candidates from the Hubble Space Telescope/ACS F606W images of NGC 4993 in the archive, using the structural parameters of the detected sources. The radial number density distribution of these candidates shows a significant central concentration around the galaxy center at the galactocentric distance <50'' , showing that they are mostly the members of NGC 4993.
Adopting the calibration of the turnover magnitudes of the globular clusters, M_V( max )=-7.58 0.11 , we derive a distance to NGC 4993, d=41.65 3.00 Mpc ( (m-M)_0=33.10 0.16 ).

NGC 4993 shows some features of recent merger including dust lanes in the central region im17,lev17 . The central velocity dispersion of NGC 4993 is as large as _v 170 im17,hjo17 , indicating that NGC 4993 is a massive galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4993 as an early-type GW170817 host whose high mass, low SFR, colour, and merger-rate estimates make DNS detection unsurprising.

quotes.quote: The spectacular detection of the counterpart of GW170817, sweeping almost the entire electromagnetic spectrum from radio to gamma-ray wavelengths abbottmultimessenger,abbottGRB,goldstein2017,savchenko2017,margutti2017,coulter2017,soares-santos2017,chornock2017,cowperthwaite2017,nicholl2017,pian2017,alexander2017 has lead to the unique identification of the host galaxy, NGC4993. This is an early-type galaxy at redshift z 0.009783 Levan2017 , which probably underwent a major merger recently, with stellar mass of 0.3-1.2 10^ 11 and metallicity in the range of 20 100 the solar metallicity Im2017 .

The black lines mark the mass-metallicity range of NGC 4993, the host galaxy of GW170817 reported by Im2017 . Although the uncertainty on the metallicity of NGC 4993 is extremely large, this galaxy seems to be remarkably metal poor with respect to the average value of the galaxies in our sample. The mass of NGC 4993 matches the mass of the most massive galaxies in our sample.

From Figure fig:GW25MpcGalaxyColours it is apparent that NGC 4993 falls in the region of the colour-magnitude diagram associated with the higher merger rate per galaxy in the local Universe. This suggests that NGC 4993 is also one of the galaxies where it is more likely to detect DNS mergers, although to quantitatively support this statement we need to include observational selection effects and to convolve them with the instrumental range of GW detectors (Artale et al., in preparation).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC4993, the low-redshift lenticular host of GW170817, to reassess Hubble-constant inference from standard sirens by comparing total, peculiar, and group velocity estimates.

quotes.quote: The presence of the electromagnetic counterparts gave a precise determination (with probability of chance association P<0.004 ; Abbott2017c ) that the host of the gravitational wave was NGC4993, a low redshift lenticular galaxy. The combination of gravitational wave and host identification then allowed for the first ever ``Standard Siren" measurement of the Hubble constant, H_ 0 , analogous to methods using ``Standard Candles" (type Ia supernovae - SN Ia; Cepheid variable stars, etc.,) and ``Standard Rulers" (e.g., Baryon Acoustic Oscillations - BAO; the Cosmic Microwave Background - CMB). This determination of the Hubble constant was made possible through the combination of cosmological luminosity distance inferred from the gravitational-wave signal, combined with the observed total and peculiar velocities of NGC4993. The luminosity distance inferred from the gravitational wave is consistent with independent measurements of the distance to NGC4993 e.g. Hjorth2017,Im2017,Cantiello2018 .

For an object such as NGC4993 (with CMB-frame redshift cz_ cmb = 3228 15 km s^ 1 ) the peculiar motion of the galaxy within its group or cluster could easily be as much as 20 of the CMB-frame velocity. Hence, the internal peculiar motion is expected to give a larger error than the error due to using the centre of the cluster as the distance (see Table tab:recvel for estimates of such a distance for NGC4993). Similarly, using the redshift of the cluster is closer to the cosmological redshift than a galaxy within the cluster will be.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4993 as GW170817's old early-type host, using its merger signatures, stellar population, neutron-star population, and dark-matter halo in PBH scenarios.

quotes.quote: NGC 4993 is a nearby ( z = 0.009680 0.000150 ) early–type galaxy with stellar mass M_ =(3.8 0.20) 10^ 10 M_ , with i band Sérsic index n = 4.0 and low asymmetry. Despite the fact that these results are consistent with a passively evolving, red and dead galaxy, the presence of shells and dust lanes indicate that it recently underwent a galaxy merger, as we describe in more detail in Section sec:merger . From the perspective of BNS formation, the association of GW170817 as a BNS merger with an old elliptical galaxy, like NGC 4993, is puzzling . The galaxy does not show recent signs of star formation, and the median inferred stellar population age is 11 Gyr .

Dark Matter Distribution The mass of the NGC 4993 DM halo is estimated to be M_ halo, NGC4993 = 194^ +120 _ 70 10^ 10 M_ . In order to compute the rates described in Section sec:merger , we need to estimate the radial DM profile for this galaxy. We assume a Navarro, Frenk and White (NFW ) density profile, and the concentration-mass relation of Ref. , which implies a concentration c_ 200 =7.52^ +2.17 _ 2.07 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4993 as GW170817's host galaxy, combining its redshift and locally weighted peculiar velocity from 6dF galaxies to improve the standard-siren Hubble constant estimate.

quotes.quote: During the search for the counterpart in different wavelengths, a transient called ``kilonova'' associated with the GW event was discovered in the optical , which enabled the identification of the host galaxy of the event: NGC 4993 . As this was the first GW multi-messenger observation, the first standard siren measurement of the distance to the source of the event was derived using the GW data and the host galaxy redshift .
We use the catalog of peculiar velocity data from the 6dF Galaxy Survey to calculate the weighted peculiar velocity of NGC 4993.

From the 6dF Galaxy Survey (6dFGSv) , we calculate the peculiar velocities ( v_p ) of the galaxies surrounding NGC 4993, the host galaxy of the binary neutron star merger GW170817. Following , we use: v_p = cz(1-10^ ) where z is the CMB frame galaxy redshift, and is the logarithmic distance ratio D_z/D_H , where D_z and D_H are the linear comoving distances of the galaxy corresponding to the observed redshift and Hubble redshift, respectively. For the complete derivation of this equation, see . To obtain the peculiar velocity of the host galaxy of the merger, we weigh the peculiar velocities of the other galaxies in the catalog according to their distance from NGC 4993 by using a 3D Gaussian kernel centered the location of NGC 4993.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4993 as the host of AT2017gfo, comparing distance estimates, recession and peculiar velocity constraints, and low dust attenuation for Hubble-constant inference.

quotes.quote: These EPM distances are consistent with earlier distance estimates to the host galaxy NGC 4993, including from surface brightness fluctuations Cantiello2018 , the fundamental plane Hjorth2017 , GW standard siren measurements Abbott2017a , and combining the GW standard siren with constraints on inclination angle from very-long-baseline interferometry Hotokezaka2019 .

The recession velocity of the host-galaxy group is well constrained: z_ CMB = 0.01110 0.00024 Abbott2017a . However, the contribution of the uncertainty on the peculiar velocity to z_ cosmic is large due to the low redshift of the host galaxy NGC 4993. Several estimates of z_ pec have been made Hjorth2017,Howlett2020, Nicolaou2020 . The most recent analysis, using a reconstruction of observed galaxy distributions from a full forward modelling of large-scale structure and large-scale velocity flow with with Bayesian Origins Reconstruction from Galaxies BORG; Jasche2019 model, determines z_ pec = 0.00124 0.00043 Mukherjee2021 .

Blanchard2017 find a similar upper limit for the total dust attenuation of the light of NGC 4993 ( A_V<0.11 mag) based on modelling the spectral energy distribution of the whole galaxy, again, consistent with our low estimate. While in this case we find the host galaxy extinction negligible compared to the Milky Way extinction, a better estimate would be useful.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SSS17a in NGC 4993, gives the host redshift, and reports nondetections of host absorption or emission lines near the merger site.

quotes.quote: SSS17a was located in the galaxy NGC 4993 , at a distance of 40 megaparsecs (Mpc) , which is an order of magnitude closer than previous gravitational wave detections. This discovery was immediately announced to the LVC, and we began a comprehensive followup campaign that extended for nearly three weeks.

The host galaxy, NGC 4993 , has a redshift of z=0.00988 . We are unable to detect any absorption lines associated with the host galaxy.This result is in agreement with the results of VLT/X-Shooter observations . Specifically, we do not detect host galaxy Na D absorption, from which we conclude that all of the extinction along the line of sight to SSS17a is located in the Milky Way.
The lack of any neutral gas in NGC 4993 near the site of the merger might indicate that SSS17a was on the near side of the host galaxy, or that NGC 4993 is a largely gas-free system.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4993 as the S0 host of GW170817, showing shell features, a recent dry merger, weak nuclear activity, and an old local stellar population.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy, NGC 4993, is an S0 galaxy at z=0.009783 . There is evidence for large, face-on spiral shells in continuum imaging, and edge-on spiral features visible in nebular emission lines. This suggests that NGC 4993 has undergone a relatively recent ( 1 Gyr) ``dry'' merger. This merger may provide the fuel for a weak active nucleus seen in Chandra imaging.

At first sight, NGC 4993 appears to be a typical S0 galaxy: it has a strong bulge component and some visible dust lanes close to the galaxy core in the HST imaging (Figure 1), suggestive of recent merger activity in an ancient population. It has a measured redshift from our MUSE data of z=0.009783 , corresponding to a distance of 42.5 Mpc assuming a Hubble expansion with H_0 = 69.6 km s ^ 1 and neglecting any peculiar velocity.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4993 as GW170817’s host, uses multiwavelength photometry for its SED, and finds a massive, low-extinction galaxy with low current star formation and old, declining stellar history.

quotes.quote: About 0.5 days after the GW trigger our group used the Dark Energy Camera on the Blanco 4 m telescope to discover an optical counterpart ( i 17.48 and z 17.59 mag) associated with the galaxy NGC 4993 at a distance of d 39.5 Mpc DECAMgcn,DECamPaper1 , which was independently discovered by dubbed SSS17a SWOPEpaper,SWOPEgcn and dubbed DLT17ck DLT40gcn . The transient is also known as AT 2017gfo.

For the purpose of modeling the host galaxy spectral energy distribution (SED), we retrieved archival observations of NGC 4993, including UV and IR photometry from the GALEX , 2MASS, and WISE catalogs via the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database.

We find a low current star formation rate (averaged over the last 100 Myr) of log(SFR _ 100 Myr / M_ yr ^ 1 ) = -2.0^ +0.4 _ 0.7 , a total mass formed in stars of log( M/M_ ) = 10.90^ +0.03 _ 0.03 , and a stellar mass of log( M_ * /M_ ) = 10.65^ +0.03 _ 0.03 (defined as the current mass in stars and stellar remnants). There is no significant dust extinction with a 95 upper limit of A_ V < 0.11 . Of particular interest here is the SFH, shown in eight temporal bins in Figure SED . We find an exponentially declining SFH with a peak star formation rate 10 Gyr ago of about 10 M_ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4993 as the normal early-type host of DLT17ck, monitored by DLT40 before the event and containing the detected kilonova offset from its center.

quotes.quote: The apparent host galaxy of DLT17ck is the normal early-type galaxy NGC 4993 see 2017GCN..21645....1A . In this paper we present the observations of the DLT40 team associated with the kilonova DLT17ck, based on our ongoing one-day cadence search. The DLT40 team was one of the initial groups reporting the discovery of the kilonova (Section sec:discovery ), and based on our light curve and an early spectrum, we show that DLT17ck resembles the expected observables of a kilonova (Section sec:dlt17ck ). The DLT40 team also has a history of observations of the NGC 4993 field during the year before the GW event (Section sec:prediscovery ).

In this work, we present the only transient we detected within either the LVC or Fermi localizations: AT 2017gfo/DLT17ck (detected in in NGC 4993). On 2017 August 17 23:49:55 UT (11.09 hours after the LVC event GW170817), we detected DLT17ck, at RA=13:09:48.09 and DEC=-23:22:53.4.6, 5.37W, 8.60S arcsec offset from the center of NGC 4993 see Figure fig:FC 2017GCN..21531....1A .

NGC 4993 is one of the galaxies monitored by the DLT40 supernova search, observed on average every 3 days from February 2017 to July 2017 (see Table tab1 ). Our images show no sign of an optical transient down to a limit of m_ 19 mag (see Figure 3), corresponding to M_ 14 mag at the adopted distance of NGC 4993.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4993 as the electromagnetic host of GW170817, deriving its Hubble-flow distance and comparing that distance to the gravitational-wave luminosity distance.

quotes.quote: Comparing the luminosity distance of GW170817 extracted under the assumption of GR to the EM-measured distance to its host galaxy, NGC 4993, we find stringent constraints on theories with gravitational leakage. We use these limits to set bounds on the number of additional non-compact spacetime dimensions and characterize properties of the modifications, such as the screening scale and the lifetime of the graviton.

To find the Hubble velocity of the host galaxy NGC 4993, we follow and correct the recessional velocity of the galaxy group to which NGC 4993 belongs, ESO-508, by its peculiar velocity. The EM observables are then the measured recessional velocity, v_r , of the group of galaxies to which NGC 4993 belongs, and the measured peculiar velocity, v_p , in the neighborhood of NGC 4993. We denote the true peculiar velocity by v_p , so that the true recessional velocity is the sum of v_H and v_p . We adopt the conservative uncertainty on v_p from , which sets the Hubble velocity to be v_H = 3017 250 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC 4993 as the S0 host of GRB 170817A/AT 2017go, describing the multiwavelength transient components and a possible microquasar-origin interpretation.

quotes.quote: GRB 170817A in NGC 4993 : GRB 170817A which was detected by Fermi-GBM on 17 August 2017 became one of the most famous short ray burst (GRB) due to its linking to a source of gravitational waves GW 170817 which had been recorded a couple seconds prior to it by LIGO 2017ApJ...848L..13A . The GRB became more famous when a bright optical transient source AT 2017go was detected in the S0 galaxy NGC 4993 within 11 hours of the GRB within the angular region defined by the large observing beams of the two instruments 2017Sci...358.1556C . It was suggested that all these were signals from the same source and since the source of gravitational waves which was localised to a region of area 31 square degrees, was linked to merging of two neutron stars at a luminosity distance of 40 Mpc as deduced from templates of gravitational wave signals, the electromagnetic signals also got linked to the same source 2017ApJ...848L..12A .

The electromagnetic signals associated with GRB 170817A consisted of three components - (1) a relatively faint short ray burst, (2) bright thermal emission at ultraviolet, optical, and infrared bands which is detected a few days after the GRB and fades by day 110. This emission component is known in literature as a kilonova and (3) a synchrotron component which dominates radio and X-ray bands at all epochs from around day 9, peaks in both bands around day 160 and then declines. A constant spectral index of 0.585 fits the synchrotron spectrum from X-ray to radio bands at all epochs 2018ApJ...856L..18M .

quotes.quote: Since the gradient of the host galaxy NGC 4993 can affect astrometry of point sources superposed on it, we removed the galaxy profile by modeling the light distribution in each 1014 1014 image with an array of 127 127 points, each of which in turn represents the sigma-clipped average value of the image over an 8 8-pixel region. We iteratively solved for the values of the representative grid by subtracting the current grid-model (interpolated with a bicubic spline) and examining the residuals within a 23 23 box about each grid-point. In this way, we converged upon a smooth version of the background. Subtracting this background from the images allows us to measure the point sources (the reference stars and GW170817) without bias from the gradient of the galaxy.
