This fact is in agreement with the probability that the galaxy NGC 4993 , host of the event GW 170817 , is an early-type galaxy with an old dominant stellar population abbott2017a ; The predicted [ Eu/Fe ] vs. [ Fe/H ] patterns in galaxies of different morphological type follow the expectations of the time-delay model, either in the case of MNS being the only Eu producers or if CC-SNe also participate to the Eu production.This means a longer plateau for [ Eu/Fe ] in the case of ellipticals with higher SFR, and a shorter plateau for irregulars with a weak SFR. (see matteucci2012 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4993 as the nearby GW170817 host galaxy, noting its 41 Mpc distance, old stellar population, and atypicality among observed short-GRB hosts.

quotes.quote: Originating in the galaxy NGC 4993 Levan17 located at a distance 41 3.1 Mpc Hjorth17 , two neutron stars in orbit about each other merged together, emitting waves not only across the electromagnetic (EM) spectrum but also in spacetime.

In the case of GW170817, find that the canonical DTD has too short merger times to make GW170817 a typical BNSM event, since NGC 4993 is a galaxy dominated by an old stellar population Blanchard17 . Fong17 also find that NGC 4993 is atypical in many ways to the observed host galaxies of short GRB events, suggesting the possibility that GW170817 may not be representative of BNSM events.

sources.summary: The review identifies NGC 4993 as the host galaxy of GW170817, adopting its surface-brightness-fluctuation distance and MUSE heliocentric redshift for the event analysis.

quotes.quote: Several different techniques give redshift-independent distance estimates of 40 Mpc for the host galaxy NGC 4993 (e.g., hjorth17 ). Here we adopt D_ L =40.7 1.4 1.9 Mpc (random and systematic uncertainties, respectively) obtained from surface brightness fluctuations in NGC 4993 cantiello18 . Time is referenced to the GW coalescence time, 2017-08-17 12:41:04.4 UTC (or MJD=57982.528523), . All photometry and spectroscopy in the figures have been corrected for E(B-V)=0.105 mag of Galactic reddening Schlafly11 . We adopt the MUSE/Very Large Telescope measurement of the heliocentric redshift of NGC 4993 z_ helio =0.0098 hjorth17,Levan17 , which gives a geocentric redshift z=0.0099 at the time of .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4993 as a validation case for radial peculiar-velocity inference, reporting consistent posterior velocities with and without added virial-motion uncertainty.

quotes.quote: To validate our algorithm, we infer the radial peculiar velocity posterior for NGC 4993, which has been studied by 2021A&A...646A..65M and references therein. When we consider only the peculiar motion of the halo, we find a posterior mean of v _p = 354 75 km s ^ 1 . When we add the peculiar velocity uncertainty due to the virial motion of the galaxy within its halo, we recover v _p = 357 84 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4993 as GW170817's host galaxy, adopting its surface-brightness-fluctuation distance and peculiar-velocity-corrected Hubble redshift for higher-dimensional-gravity constraints.

quotes.quote: GW170817 was followed up by more than 70 terrestrial and space-based EM observatories , which traced its source to the host galaxy, NGC 4993.
A simultaneous measurement of surface brightness fluctuations of the host galaxy, NGC 4993, yielded an EM luminosity distance, N (40.7, 2.4 ) Mpc .

Note this is the predicted redshift due to the Hubble expansion at the distance of NGC4993, the host galaxy of GW170817, after correcting for the (significant) peculiar velocity of NGC4993 -- which can be estimated from a reconstruction of the large-scale peculiar velocity field in its vicinity, as e.g. derived from analysis of all-sky galaxy redshift surveys combined with redshift-independent distance indicator information .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4993 as the S0 host galaxy of the GW170817 kilonova, using its distance and redshift for Hubble-constant measurements and follow-up localization.

quotes.quote: Further, the multi-wavelength EM data shows that, the merger caused a sGRB (GRB 170817A) 2017ApJ...848L..13A,2017ApJ...848L..14G in a galaxy named NGC 4993, which was followed by a kilonova powered by the radioactive decay of -process nuclei synthesized in the ejecta 2017ApJ...848L..12A .

The first of its kind joint analysis of the GW170817 provides us the unique opportunity of measuring the absolute distance to the source directly from the GW measurements event ( 40 Mpc), and hence the distance to the galaxy NGC 4993.
The Swope supernova survey telescope detected the kilonova Swope_2017 and located it at NGC 4993, an S0 galaxy at a distance of 40 mega-parsecs.

gmw_00000034.name: NGC 7619
gmw_00000034.overview: NGC 7619 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1810.00710] in the Pegasus I group [arXiv:1810.00710] that hosts a low-activity nucleus [arXiv:1904.02446] and exhibits regular stellar rotation [arXiv:1306.1124, arXiv:2201.07838]. It features an extended X-ray halo with an asymmetric tail [arXiv:1810.00710, arXiv:1904.02446] and an elongated ionized gas filament [arXiv:2201.07838].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7619 photometry to demonstrate its Bayesian orientation method, finding likelihood-dominated, well-constrained orientation probabilities and a maximum near specific viewing angles.

quotes.quote: The observed data of NGC 7619 is _ in = 0.28 _ out = 0.19 , _ d = 4^ o .0 at R_ in =10.55 arcsec and R_ out = 45.71 arcsec. Effective radius R_ e of NGC 7619 is 32.0 arcsec. This data is obtained from R-band photometry of Franx et al. (1989). While applying this data to calculate the probability of orientation, we consider the uncertainty of 0.02 in ellipticities, and the uncertainty of 1^ o .0 in position angles both at R_ in and R_ out . Figure 2 presents the plot of the posterior probability distribution P of the orientation as a function of polar coordinates ( ^ ' , ^ ' ) of the line of sight.

We find that the 1 region occupies 27.9 of the total parameter space. The probability is likelihood dominated and is well constrained. The location of the maximum probability is at ^ ' = 40^ o .5, ^ ' = 106^ o .5 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 7619 as an intermediate rotator, finding steep h3 anti-correlation, a fast-rotator-like inner angular-momentum profile, and likely template mismatch in older comparisons.

quotes.quote: There is only very minimal v_ rot h_ 2n + 1 (anti-) correlation and odd moment-to-moment oscillations, with the notable exception being the intermediate rotator NGC 7619, which shows steep v_ rot h_3 anti-correlation, which also features prominently in the galaxy's kinematic maps (see Fig. fig:SRkinmap ). None of the slow + intermediate rotating galaxies have dynamically cold components, |v_ rot / | > 1 .

NGC 7619, one of the intermediate rotating galaxies, has a profile perfectly consistent with that of the fast rotators in our sample for small /r_e , before shallowing out to a profile that is more similar to the slow rotators, but with overall larger at the same /r_e . Here, the ``intermediate'' classification seems to be particularly apt.

NGC 7619 : Along the major axis, within 5 the kinematics of Pu2010 match ours well (see bottom left panel of Fig. fig:ngc5328Profile ). At larger radii, and along the minor axis, the and h_4 measured by Pu2010 (see bottom right panel of Fig. fig:ngc5328Profile ) are larger than our measurements. Furthermore, their v_ rot and profiles appear less symmetric about the center of the galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 7619 is one of Pegasus I's two dominant giant ellipticals, a radio source with peculiar morphology, extended X-ray emission, an asymmetric tail, and Chandra cold fronts linked to subgroup merging.

quotes.quote: The Pegasus I group is dominated by two giant elliptical galaxies of similar luminosities: NGC 7619 ( V=11.06 mag) and NGC 7626 ( V=11.08 mag) 1992yCat.7137....0D . Both galaxies are radio sources and show morphological peculiarities.

NGC 7619, in turn, is a strong and extended X-ray source with a long asymmetric X-ray tail in the southwest direction 2008ApJ...688..931K,2009ApJ...696.1431R . This tail has been interpreted as due to the infall of this galaxy into Pegasus I. In addition, cold fronts detected in each of these dominant galaxies using Chandra images 2009ApJ...696.1431R support the scenario of a major merger of two subgroups.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7619 as regularly rotating with a major-axis dispersion decline, flat-topped LOSVDs, and extended kinematic data used for modeling.

quotes.quote: Four galaxies exhibit regular rotation: NGC 1374, NGC 4751, NGC 6861 and NGC 7619. In these galaxies, h_3 is anti-correlated with the rotational velocity, matching a widely observed trend (e.g. Bender-94,Krajnovic-08,Krajnovic-11,Fabricius-12 ).

In NGC 7619, the velocity dispersion profile decreases more steeply along the major axis than along the minor axis and the majority of the LOSVDs are flat-topped as indicated by negative values in the h_4 map.
For this galaxy, we used the kinematic data from Pu-10 who provided V , , h_3 and h_4 out to 60 arcsec, almost twice the R_e , along both the major and minor axes.

sources.summary: NGC 7619 shows large-scale stellar rotation, an elongated NE-SW ionized filament, a shallow velocity gradient, and a fainter NE filament.

quotes.quote: The stellar velocity fields reveal that most of the sources in our sample (10/18 sources, e.g., NGC 940, NGC 924, NGC 1453, NGC 4169, NGC 978, NGC 584, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 7619 and NGC 6658) show a clear large-scale rotation pattern in their stellar light.

NGC 7619 -- host an elongated (12 ) filament along the NE--SW axis. It has a shallow velocity gradient of + 20 km s ^ 1 -40 km s ^ 1 . There is a fainter filament located 3.6 kpc (14 arcsec ) NE of the center moving with a projected velocity of roughly 90 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7619 LOSVDs as an example, successfully fits most of its MUSE and SINFONI coverage, and treats its ordered velocity field as axisymmetry-friendly.

quotes.quote: Combining the kinematic data In Figure fig:exampleLOSVDs we show, as an example, all the LOSVDs of NGC 7619, including both MUSE and SINFONI LOSVDs, divided into quadrants.

NGC 7619 (and NGC 5516) : While we can successfully fit the MUSE and SINFONI kinematics for NGC 7619 for the majority of our spatial coverage, there are some small black problems at the largest and smallest radii of the MUSE data (the SINFONI data is reproduced well over the full SINFONI coverage, see Figure fig:radKin ). At large radii ( > 20 ) the h_4 of our models rises towards the edges of the MUSE FOV, whereas the MUSE data appears to follow the opposite trend. Within 2 , our models underpredict the dispersion of the MUSE data (while reproducing all of the SINFONI data correctly).

sources.summary: The paper uses deep VLT I-band surface-brightness-fluctuation observations of NGC 7619 in Pegasus to measure its distance scale and infer H0 near 68 km/s/Mpc.

quotes.quote: Occhialini, Via Bassini 15, 20133 Milano, Italy European Southern Observatory, Karl-Schwarzschild-Strasse 2, 85748 Garching, Germany Received June 28, 2002; We have measured the Hubble constant H_0 in NGC 564 at cz 5800 km s ^ 1 and in NGC 7619 at cz 3700 km s ^ 1 with deep I--band Surface Brightness Fluctuation distance measurements at the ESO Very Large Telescope (VLT). We obtain H_0 = 70 7 6 km s ^ 1 /Mpc for NGC 564 and H_0 = 68 6 6 for NGC 7619.
Deep I--band SBF observations were obtained for two galaxies : NGC 564, in Abell 194, at cz 5800 km s ^ 1 , and NGC 7619 in the Pegasus cluster at cz 3700 km s ^ 1 .
For NGC 7619, the nights of observation were on 5 October 2000 for the I--band and the 3 November 2000 for the V--band.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 7619 has a small alpha value; refitting with the authors circularized profile gives different core-Sersic parameters and a larger rms.

quotes.quote: NGC 3091 and NGC 7619 show small values in , i.e. < 2 . Dullo-12 produce a best-fitting \ model of NGC 7619 by keeping constant at 5, which enforces a rather sharp transition from \ to power-law profiles. Doing this exercise using our circularized profile of NGC 7619 results in a smaller n (7.71), larger r_b (0.73 ) and (0.47) and a larger rms (0.033).

sources.summary: The review compares NGC 7619 with NGC 7626, describing it as an elliptical with a more extended X-ray halo, nuclear-confined radio emission, and a low-activity nucleus.

quotes.quote: In the pair NGC 7626 and NGC 7619, both of which are classified as elliptical galaxies and have comparable optical sizes, a more extended X-ray halo is detected around NGC 7619 in which radio emission is confined to the nucleus whereas the diffuse X-ray emission around NGC 7626 is relatively compact but it shows bright radio jets and lobes extending to several tens of kpcs beyond the optical galaxy and the X-ray halo indicating the presence of a vigorous active nucleus 2009ApJ...696.1431R . A cloud of gas of temperature of about 0.75 keV seems to be associated with both the galaxies while the hot thermal gas that is observed to pervade the space beyond the galaxies shows higher temperatures 2009ApJ...696.1431R . These observation can be used to infer the X-ray halo around NGC 7619 is formed when the galaxy lost most of its gas which evaporated into the hot tenous thermal gas and only a small amount of the interstellar gas was channelled inwards to the supermassive black hole so that it hosts a low-activity nucleus which has not resulted in a large radio structure.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 7619 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: We find the majority (10/18) of our BGEs to be regular rotators (RR): NGC 584, NGC 924, NGC 940, NGC 978, NGC 1453, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4169, NGC 6658, and NGC 7619. Here, we find that all of the h_ 3 versus V/ anti-correlation slopes of our fast rotating BGEs are around -0.1 as expected, with the steepest being NGC 940 (--0.15) and NGC 7619 (--0.16). NGC 940 has _ e 0.6 , therefore NGC 7619 (with a fairly low _ e 0.14 ) is the only BGE that can potentially be similar to the Class D galaxies in Naab2014 .

gmw_00000035.name: NGC 7626
gmw_00000035.overview: NGC 7626 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1810.00710, arXiv:1310.2770] in the Pegasus I group [arXiv:1810.00710] that hosts a radio-loud [arXiv:0806.3470] LINER nucleus [arXiv:0806.3470, arXiv:1310.2770] and symmetric radio jets [arXiv:1103.1364, arXiv:1810.00710]. The galaxy features a counter-rotating core [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256], a nuclear dust lane [arXiv:1810.00710], and a shell system likely created by a past minor merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0703588, arXiv:astro-ph/0606385].

sources.summary: NGC 7626 is linked to a past minor merger; its shell colors, brightness, morphology, radio-galaxy status, and dust lane/nuclear ring point to a gas-rich intruder scenario.

quotes.quote: NGC 7626 Its core kinematics balcells and the fact that it has bright globular clusters (Paper I) indicate a (minor) merger event which happened one or several Gyr ago. This is the first time that shell colours and brightness have been obtained for this galaxy. A shell on the East side was detected by jedr2 ; this shell lies outside our field of view. Another possible shell lying on the S.W. side, was detected by ft . This shell is also seen in our data.

Frame 3 (after 4 Gyr) of this Figure looks very similar to the NGC 7626 shell system. The zero rotational momentum encounter should create phase wrapped shells which is probably evidenced by the radial shell shapes (see Section 5.2). Further support for a disk intruder galaxy comes from the shell colours.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7626 as a Pegasus I elliptical with companion NGC 7619, counter-rotating core, warped dust lane, nuclear emission, and double-sided radio lobes.

quotes.quote: NGC 7626 NGC 7626 has a nearby higher luminosity companion NGC 7619 which is at 6.8' (90 kpc). They are the two brightest ellipticals in the Pegasus I cluster. NGC 7626 has a counter rotating core (Forbes 1995; Carollo 1997). There is a small warped dust lane (diameter 230 pc/ 1.0'' ) across the nucleus. Interestingly the central part of the dust lane is perpendicular to the radio jet while the warped outer part aligns with the major axis of the galaxy.
There is an unresolved nuclear emission peak and low-level emission gas is seen along the dust lane. NGC 7626 has double-sided radio lobes (Xu 1999a).
Forbes Thomson (1992) reported excess light in the galaxy which might be due to tidal interaction with NGC 7619.

sources.summary: The paper's mock-lens analysis finds NGC7626 is a massive deflector whose flux-ratio anomaly is driven partly by nearby globular clusters, a background galaxy, and other small-scale structures.

quotes.quote: NGC7626: The most massive cusp mock lens ( _* = 274 km sec ^ 1 ) with a significant R_ cusp anomaly. NGC7626 is surrounded by globular clusters and luminous satellites.

One background galaxy is visible to the lower left, and it induces a flux ratio anomaly in the fold configuration (see the S2/M1 ratio in Figure fig:fluxratios ), although it is too far from the cusp images to be responsible for the R_ cusp anomaly and does not affect the merging pair in the fold configuration. There are two structures between the M2 and S2 images, seen clearly in the convergence map (Figure fig:fluxratios ) and in the map of the magnification surface (Figures fig:magmaps_NGC7626 and fig:magmaps_beforeafter ) which visibly perturb the critical curve.

After removing these small scale structures, which our normalization procedure assigned convergence equivalent to that produced by a 10^7 M_ perturber, we find that the R_ cusp anomaly shrinks in magnitude to 0.19 from 0.26. In the context of Figure fig:poserr_vs_Rcuspfold , this suggests it could be accounted for by an SIE model. We therefore conclude that the main source of anomaly in this system is due to structure in the deflector on scales smaller than the image separation.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 7626 as a radio-loud additional AGN candidate correlated with a cosmic ray, with a possible L2 nucleus and low inferred bolometric luminosity.

quotes.quote: Only two have published optical line fluxes, WKK 4374 (IGR J14515-5542) and NGC 7626. The former, WKK 4374, is a narrow line Seyfert (S2) with L_ bol = 1.9 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 and _ bol = 0.46 ; intriguingly, it correlates with a previously uncorrelated UHECR (Auger year, day = 2007, 186; E=64 EeV). The latter is a radio-loud LINER with L_ bol = 1.2 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 , _ bol = 0.002 . Two of the UHECRs whose VCV-correlated galaxies were found here to not be identifiable AGNs, (2006, 185; 83 EeV) and (2007, 84; 64 EeV), correlate with the new ``MSPC" source candidates NGC 7626 and NGC 2907 respectively, although a spectrum is needed to determine whether the latter meets optical AGN criteria.

Furthermore, two of the additional AGNs, NGC 7626 and NGC 2907, correlate with two CRs, (2006, 185; E=83 EeV) and (2007, 84; E=64 EeV), respectively, whose VCV counterparts were shown in this paper to be non-nuclear dominated emission line galaxies.

NGC 7626 : This is a radio loud galaxy. Ho1997a determine it to have a possible L2 nucleus (the classification is characterized as highly uncertain), with an [ OIII ] luminosity of 3.3 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 (100 uncertainty), which implies a bolometric luminosity of 1.2 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , giving _ bol = 0.0017 . Since some correlated galaxies are chance coincidences, this may be one of them.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 7626 as an elliptical with compact diffuse X-ray emission but bright radio jets and lobes, interpreting it as a vigorously fed active nucleus.

quotes.quote: In the pair NGC 7626 and NGC 7619, both of which are classified as elliptical galaxies and have comparable optical sizes, a more extended X-ray halo is detected around NGC 7619 in which radio emission is confined to the nucleus whereas the diffuse X-ray emission around NGC 7626 is relatively compact but it shows bright radio jets and lobes extending to several tens of kpcs beyond the optical galaxy and the X-ray halo indicating the presence of a vigorous active nucleus 2009ApJ...696.1431R . A cloud of gas of temperature of about 0.75 keV seems to be associated with both the galaxies while the hot thermal gas that is observed to pervade the space beyond the galaxies shows higher temperatures 2009ApJ...696.1431R . These observation can be used to infer the X-ray halo around NGC 7619 is formed when the galaxy lost most of its gas which evaporated into the hot tenous thermal gas and only a small amount of the interstellar gas was channelled inwards to the supermassive black hole so that it hosts a low-activity nucleus which has not resulted in a large radio structure. On the other hand, in NGC 7626 which hosts a large double radio source but a smaller X-ray halo than NGC 7619, it could be that most of its interstellar medium was channeled inwards and fed the central black hole which in turn has ejected a large fraction of this in the form of positron-electron jets and optical line emitting gas.

The observed X-ray halo in this galaxy might have formed from the galaxy losing some of its interstellar medium or it could have formed from isobaric expansion of the optical line emitting gas into hot tenous X-ray emitting gas. From the widely different sizes of the soft X-ray halo around NGC 7626 and the radio double, it appears more likely that the current X-ray halo is formed from the interstellar medium and contribution from the ejected matter from the black hole is yet to append to it.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7626 as a shell elliptical with a complex globular-cluster color histogram and abnormally luminous clusters, interpreted as possible young clusters from a recent minor merger.

quotes.quote: In NGC 7626, we find in addition a population of abnormally luminous clusters at M_I=-12.5 . In NGC 2865 we find an unusually blue population, which may also be young. In NGC1344 and NGC474 the red cluster population is marginally detected. The radial surface density profiles are more flattened than the galaxy light in the cores. As already known, in NGC3923, which has a high S_N of 5.6, the radial density distribution is more shallower than the diffuse galaxy light. The clusters in NGC 2865 and NGC 7626 provide evidence for formation of a population associated with a recent merger.

For NGC 7626 KMM gives maxima at 0.92 and 1.13; the bimodal nature was already observed by earlier, but less deep, observations ( ). However inspection and the output of KMM show that the simple bimodal form is not a good fit, instead the histogram shows a broad flat peak. The middle of this peak ( (V-I) 1.0 ) is filled by exceptionally bright ( M_I < -11 ) and probably young GCs (see Section sec:recentGCs ).
We conclude that NGC 7626 appears to have some young GCs, probably created in a recent (2-5 Gyr old) minor merger.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7626 as a bright Pegasus elliptical hosting an extended WAT radio galaxy with symmetric jets, tails, and merger-related X-ray structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 7626 NGC 7626 is one of the two brightest ellipticals in the Pegasus galaxy group. The dominant galaxy, NGC 7619, lies 100 kpc from NGC 7626 in the plane of the sky. A radio point-source is associated with NGC 7619, while NGC 7626 hosts a very extended radio source with symmetric jets (Jenkins 1982; Birkinshaw Davis 1985). Our GMRT images of the NGC 7619/NGC 7626 system are shown in Fig. fig:ngc7626 as contours on the POSS-2 and Chandra images. The high sensitivity of our observations allows us to image the WAT radio galaxy NGC 7626 in its whole extent, including the low-surface brightness tails at the end of the jets.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7626 as a slow rotator with significant minor-axis rotation and models only the radial range bounded by uncertain outer reversal and inner turnover.

quotes.quote: NGC 7626. This is another slow rotator. The only significant rotation is on the minor axis. Outside of 20 the rotation curve seems to reverse, but the errors are so large that the reversal is not statistically significant. We model the data only inside 20 . A turnover radius in the rotation curve on the major axis at approximately 5 sets the inner radius.

sources.summary: NGC 7626 is described as a dominant Pegasus I giant elliptical and radio source with symmetric jets/lobes, shells, dust lane, central X-ray source, and cold fronts tied to subgroup merging.

quotes.quote: The Pegasus I group is dominated by two giant elliptical galaxies of similar luminosities: NGC 7619 ( V=11.06 mag) and NGC 7626 ( V=11.08 mag) 1992yCat.7137....0D . Both galaxies are radio sources and show morphological peculiarities. NGC 7626 presents symmetric radio-jets/lobes on each side of its central core, outer and inner shells, a nuclear dust lane, a compact X-ray source at its center, among other features 1985ApJ...291...32B,1988ApJ...330L..87J,1993A&A...279..376B,1997A&A...318..361T .
In addition, cold fronts detected in each of these dominant galaxies using Chandra images 2009ApJ...696.1431R support the scenario of a major merger of two subgroups.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7626 off the model grid, interprets it as likely old and metal rich, and notes a dust lane without irregular dust patches.

quotes.quote: NGC 7626 -- This galaxy's data are significantly off the grid of models. A general feature that can be recognized from the location of this galaxy in the SBF versus color panels, is that the stellar population is very likely old, and metal rich, as also found by denicolo05 . The galaxy shows the presence of a dust lane, however we do not recognize irregular dust patches (neither from the V-band image, nor from the B--band images also available from the ACS archive) which might lead to mark as unreliable the SBF value for this galaxy.
